choices.justification: Disabling the compiler option in the production machine is not directly related to controlling the risk of emergency changes. It is more focused on preventing unauthorized code modifications. Although access controls are important, they should be implemented alongside proper change management processes.

questions.text: An auditor conducting an audit of controls in an IT environment will perform substantive tests for the controls implemented after:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's change, configuration, release, and patch management programs

choices.label: A
choices.text: the control is automated using technical tools.
choices.justification: Automated controls are implemented to mitigate control risk; substantive testing may not be performed because automated controls follow the defined processes as per control design.

choices.label: B
choices.text: the original control design has been changed during implementation.
choices.justification: If the control designed to mitigate risk has been changed while implemented, it may fail the compliance test. The auditor needs to evaluate if the changes continue to mitigate the risk and then perform substantive tests.

choices.label: C
choices.text: confirming the control is designed to mitigate risk.
choices.justification: The auditor tests the implementation of a control as per its design. If it passes that test, further testing may occur.

choices.label: D
choices.text: confirming the control is implemented as designed.
choices.justification: Substantive testing is performed after compliance testing that verifies that the control is implemented as designed. If the compliance test fails, the auditor may not perform substantive testing.

questions.text: An information systems (IS) auditor is assisting in the design of the emergency change control procedures for an organization with a limited budget. Which of the following recommendations BEST helps to establish accountability for the system support personnel?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's change, configuration, release, and patch management programs

choices.label: A
choices.text: Production access is granted to the individual support ID when needed.
choices.justification: Production access should be controlled and monitored to ensure separation of duties. During an emergency change, a user who normally does not have access to production may require access. The best process to ensure accountability within the production system is to have the information security team create a production support group and add the user ID to that group to promote the change. When the change is complete the ID can be removed from the group. This process ensures that activity in production is linked to the specific ID that was used to make the change.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Developers use a firefighter ID to promote code to production.
choices.justification: Some organizations may use a firefighter ID, which is a generic/shared ID, to promote changes to production. When needed, the developer can use this ID to access production. It may still be difficult to determine who made the change; therefore, although this process is commonly used, the use of a production support ID is a better choice.

choices.label: C
choices.text: A dedicated user promotes emergency changes to production.
choices.justification: Having a dedicated user who promotes changes to production in an emergency is ideal but is generally not cost-effective and may not be realistic for emergency changes.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Emergency changes are authorized prior to promotion.
choices.justification: Emergency changes are, by definition, unauthorized changes. Approvals usually are obtained following promotion of the change to production. All changes should be auditable, and that can best be accomplished by having a user ID added/removed to the production support group as needed.

questions.text: The application systems of an organization using open-source software have no single recognized developer producing patches. Which of the following is the MOST secure way of updating open-source software?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's change, configuration, release, and patch management programs

choices.label: A
choices.text: Rewrite the patches and apply them.
choices.justification: Rewriting the patches and applying them requires skilled resources and time to rewrite the patches, making it less practical and secure.

choices.label: B
choices.text: the code and application of available patches.
choices.justification: Code review is possible, but thorough testing is necessary before applying the patches.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Develop in-house patches.
choices.justification: Developing in-house patches may not be the most secure option if the IT department lacks the necessary skills and resources.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Identify and test suitable patches before applying them.
choices.justification: Suitable patches from the existing developers should be selected and tested before applying them.

questions.text: An enterprise uses privileged accounts to process configuration changes for mission-critical applications. Which of the following is the BEST and appropriate control to limit the risk in such a situation?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's change, configuration, release, and patch management programs

choices.label: A
choices.text: Ensure that audit trails are accurate and specific.
choices.justification: Audit trails are a detective control and, in many cases, can be altered by those with privileged access.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Ensure that personnel have adequate training.
choices.justification: Staff proficiency is important and good training may be somewhat of a deterrent, but supervisory approval and review is the best choice.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Ensure that personnel background checks are performed for critical personnel.
choices.justification: Performing background checks is a very basic control and will not effectively prevent or detect errors or malfeasance.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Ensure that supervisory approval and review are performed for critical changes.
choices.justification: Supervisory approval and review of critical changes by accountable managers are necessary to prevent and detect unauthorized changes. They ensure separation of duties and safeguard against unauthorized attempts by employees.

questions.text: Which of the following is the MOST efficient and sufficiently reliable way to test the design effectiveness of a change control process?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's change, configuration, release, and patch management programs

choices.label: A
choices.text: Test a sample population of change requests.
choices.justification: Testing a sample population of changes is a test of compliance and operating effectiveness to ensure that users submitted the proper documentation/requests. It does not test the effectiveness of the design.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Test a sample of authorized changes.
choices.justification: Testing changes that have been authorized may not provide sufficient assurance of the entire process because it does not test the elements of the process related to authorization or detect changes that bypassed the controls.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Interview personnel in charge of the change control process.
choices.justification: Interviewing personnel in charge of the change control process is not as effective as a walk-through of the change controls process because people may know the process but not follow it.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Perform an end-to-end walk-through of the process.
choices.justification: Observation is the best and most effective method to test changes to ensure that the process is effectively designed.

questions.text: An information systems (IS) auditor has discovered that a new patch is available for an application, but the IT department has decided that the patch is not needed because other security controls are in place. What should the IS auditor recommend?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's change, configuration, release, and patch management programs

choices.label: A
choices.text: Apply the patch only after it has been thoroughly tested.
choices.justification: Applying a patch without first performing a risk assessment might be a waste of resources if it is determined that the application is not mission critical.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Implement a host-based intrusion detection system (IDS).
choices.justification: Implementing a host-based intrusion detection system (IDS) is a valid control; however, it may not address vulnerabilities within the application.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Modify the firewall rules to further protect the application server.
choices.justification: Modifying the firewall rules may help to mitigate the risk of a security incident; however, first, the risk related to the patch needs to be determined.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Assess the overall risk, then recommend whether to deploy the patch.
choices.justification: Although it is important to ensure that systems are properly patched, a risk assessment needs to be performed to determine the likelihood and probability of the vulnerability being exploited. Therefore, the patch is applied only if the risk of circumventing the existing security controls is great enough to warrant it.

questions.text: An information systems (IS) auditor is carrying out a system configuration review. Which of the following is the BEST evidence in support of the current system configuration settings?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's change, configuration, release, and patch management programs

choices.label: A
choices.text: System configuration values that are imported to a spreadsheet by the system administrator
choices.justification: Evidence that is not system-generated information can be modified before it is presented to an information systems (IS) auditor. Therefore, it may not be as reliable as evidence that is obtained by the IS auditor. For example, a system administrator can change the settings or modify the graphic image before taking a screenshot.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Standard report with configuration values that are retrieved from the system by the information systems (IS) auditor
choices.justification: Evidence that is obtained directly from the source by an IS auditor is more reliable than information that is provided by a system administrator or a business owner because the IS auditor does not have a vested interest in the outcome of the audit.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Dated screenshot of the system configuration settings that are made available by the system administrator
choices.justification: The rules may be modified by the administrator prior to taking the screenshot; therefore, this is not the best evidence.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Annual review of approved system configuration values by the business owner
choices.justification: The annual review provided by a business owner may not reflect current information.

questions.text: The information systems (IS) auditor observes that the latest security-related software patches for a mission-critical system were released two months ago, but IT personnel have not yet installed the patches. The IS auditor should:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's change, configuration, release, and patch management programs

choices.label: A
choices.text: review the patch management policy and determine the risk associated with this condition.
choices.justification: the patch management policy and determining whether the IT department is compliant with the policies will detect whether the policies are appropriate and what risk is associated with current practices.

choices.label: B
choices.text: recommend that IT systems personnel test and then install the patches immediately.
choices.justification: Although there may be instances in which the patch is an urgent fix for a serious security issue, IT may have made the determination that the risk to system stability is greater than the risk identified by the software vendor who issued the patch. Therefore, the time frame selected by IT may be appropriate.

choices.label: C
choices.text: recommend that patches be applied every month or immediately upon release.
choices.justification: Although keeping critical systems properly patched helps to ensure that they are secure, the requirement for a precise timetable to patch systems may create other issues if patches are improperly tested prior to implementation. Therefore, this is not the correct answer.

choices.label: D
choices.text: take no action, because the IT processes related to patch management appear to be adequate.
choices.justification: Even if the information systems (IS) auditor concludes that the patch management process is adequate, the observation related to the time delay in applying patches should be reported.

questions.text: During an audit of a small enterprise, the information systems (IS) auditor noted that the IS director has superuser-privilege access that allows the director to process requests for changes to the application access roles (access types). Which of the following should the IS auditor recommend?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's change, configuration, release, and patch management programs

choices.label: A
choices.text: Implement a properly documented process for application role change requests.
choices.justification: The information systems (IS) auditor should recommend implementation of a process that can prevent or detect improper changes from being made to the major application roles. The application role change request process should start and be approved by the business owner; then, the IS director can make the changes to the application.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Hire additional staff to provide a separation of duties for application role changes.
choices.justification: Although it is preferred that a strict separation of duties be adhered to and that additional staff be recruited, this practice is not always possible in small enterprises. The IS auditor must look at recommended alternative processes.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Implement an automated process for changing application roles.
choices.justification: An automated process for managing application roles may not be practical to prevent improper changes being made by the IS director, who also has the most privileged access to the application.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Document the current procedure in detail and make it available on the enterprise intranet.
choices.justification: Making the existing process available on the enterprise intranet would not provide any value to protect the system.

questions.text: Emergency changes that bypass the normal change control process are MOST acceptable if:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's change, configuration, release, and patch management programs

choices.label: A
choices.text: management reviews and approves the changes after they have occurred.
choices.justification: Because management cannot always be available when a system failure occurs, it is acceptable for changes to be reviewed and approved within a reasonable time period after they occur.

choices.label: B
choices.text: the changes are reviewed by a peer at the time of the change.
choices.justification: Although peer review provides some accountability, management should review and approve all changes, even if that review and approval must occur after the fact.

choices.label: C
choices.text: the changes are documented in the change control system by the operations department.
choices.justification: Documenting the event does not replace the need for a review and approval process to occur.

choices.label: D
choices.text: management has preapproved all emergency changes.
choices.justification: It is not a good control practice for management to ignore its responsibility by preapproving all emergency changes in advance without reviewing them. Unauthorized changes can then be made without management knowledge.

questions.text: A clerk changed the interest rate for a loan on a master file. The rate entered is outside the normal range for such a loan. Which of the following controls is MOST effective in providing reasonable assurance that the change was authorized?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's change, configuration, release, and patch management programs

choices.label: A
choices.text: The system will not process the change until the clerk’s manager confirms the change by entering an approval code.
choices.justification: Requiring an approval code by a manager prevents or detects the use of an unauthorized interest rate.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The system generates a weekly report listing all rate exceptions and the report is reviewed by the clerk’s manager.
choices.justification: A weekly report informs the manager after the fact that a change was made, thereby making it possible for transactions to use an unauthorized rate prior to management review. Although this control provides visibility into rate exceptions, it does not prevent or enforce authorization in real time. By relying solely on periodic review, there is no immediate assurance that the change was authorized before it was processed.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The system requires the clerk to enter an approval code.
choices.justification: Having a clerk enter an approval code does not provide separation of duties and does not prevent the clerk from entering an unauthorized rate change.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The system displays a warning message to the clerk.
choices.justification: A warning message alerts the clerk in case the change was being made in error but does not prevent the clerk from entering an unauthorized rate change.

questions.text: The MOST effective audit procedure in determining if unauthorized changes have been made to production code is to:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's change, configuration, release, and patch management programs

choices.label: A
choices.text: examine the change control system records and trace them forward to object code files.
choices.justification: Examining the change control system records can help identify authorized changes, but it may not directly address the detection of unauthorized changes.

choices.label: B
choices.text: review access control permissions operating within the production program libraries.
choices.justification: access control permissions can help prevent unauthorized changes, but it may not detect changes that have already occurred.

choices.label: C
choices.text: examine object code to find instances of changes and trace them back to change control records.
choices.justification: The procedure of examining object code files to establish instances of code changes and tracing these back to change control system records is a substantive test that directly addresses the risk of unauthorized code changes.

choices.label: D
choices.text: review change approved designations established within the change control system.
choices.justification: change approved designations can help identify authorized changes, but it may not directly detect unauthorized changes.

questions.text: Which of the following processes should an information systems (IS) auditor recommend to assist in the recording of baselines for software releases?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's change, configuration, release, and patch management programs

choices.label: A
choices.text: user acceptance testing (UAT)
choices.justification: Although conducting user acceptance testing (UAT) is an important process in software development and release, it is not directly related to the recording of baselines for software releases. UAT may occur after the baselines have been established and serve as a validation step to ensure that the software meets the desired criteria.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Backup and recovery
choices.justification: Backup and recovery of the configuration are important, but not used to create the baseline.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Incident management
choices.justification: Incident management will determine how to respond to an adverse event but is not related to recording baseline configurations.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Configuration management
choices.justification: The configuration management process may include automated tools that provide an automated recording of software release baselines. If the new release fails, the baseline will provide a point to which to return.

questions.text: Which of the following is the MOST likely reason an organization implements an emergency change to an application using the emergency change control process?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's change, configuration, release, and patch management programs

choices.label: A
choices.text: The application owner requested new functionality.
choices.justification: Requests for new functionality by the application owner generally follow normal change control procedures, unless they have an impact on the business function.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Changes are developed using an agile methodology.
choices.justification: The agile system development methodology breaks down projects into short time-boxed iterations. Each iteration focuses on developing end-to-end functionality from user interface to data storage for the intended architecture. However, the release does not need to follow emergency release procedures unless there is a significant impact on operations.

choices.label: C
choices.text: There is a high probability of a significant impact on operations.
choices.justification: Emergency releases are urgent fixes that are implemented to prevent significant user downtime, following specific procedures.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The operating system vendor has released a security patch.
choices.justification: Operating system security patches are applied after testing, and, therefore, there is no need for an emergency release.

questions.text: Vendors have released patches fixing security flaws in their software. Which of the following should an information systems (IS) auditor recommend in this situation?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's change, configuration, release, and patch management programs

choices.label: A
choices.text: Assess the impact of patches prior to installation.
choices.justification: The effect of installing the patch should be immediately evaluated, and installation should occur based on the results of the evaluation. There are numerous cases where a patch from one vendor has affected other systems; therefore, testing the patches as much as possible before rolling them out to the entire organization is necessary.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Ask the vendors for a new software version with all fixes included.
choices.justification: New software versions with all fixes included are not always available, and a full installation can be time consuming.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Install the security patch immediately.
choices.justification: To install the patch without knowing what it might affect can easily cause problems. The installation of a patch may also affect system availability; therefore, the patch should be rolled out at a time that is acceptable to the business.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Decline to deal with these vendors in the future.
choices.justification: Declining to deal with vendors does not take care of the flaw and may severely limit service options.

questions.text: Which of the following BEST helps an information systems (IS) auditor in ensuring that automated data conversion from an old system to a new system has been completed successfully?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's log management program

choices.label: A
choices.text: Operator reports
choices.justification: Operator reports are manual logs of errors identified by the computer operator.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Exception reports
choices.justification: Exception reports are automated reports that identify errors encountered during processing, including transactions that are not converted automatically.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Control totals
choices.justification: Control totals help in ensuring that data has been converted completely but may not identify a specific record that was not converted.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Application logs
choices.justification: Application logs provide errors encountered during application execution.

domain: Information Systems Operations and Business Resilience

questions.text: Which of the following is a KEY benefit of using version control systems in configuration and release management?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's change, configuration, release, and patch management programs

choices.label: A
choices.text: Manages complexity in code management
choices.justification: Version control systems provide structure and organization to code repositories, making it easier to manage changes, track versions and collaborate effectively; however, tracking changes is the key benefit of using version control systems in configuration and release management.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Eases collaboration with multiple developers
choices.justification: Version control systems facilitate collaboration among multiple developers by providing a centralized platform for code sharing, version tracking and merging changes. They enable concurrent development, branching and merging of code, enhancing collaboration rather than making it difficult. However, tracking changes is essential in case of operational issues detected later.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Limits accessibility for developers
choices.justification: Version control systems offer access controls so developers cannot arbitrarily access code libraries and make untraceable changes. It is complementary to tracking code changes.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Provides ability to track and revert changes
choices.justification: Using version control systems enables the tracking of code changes, including who made the changes, when they were made and what modifications were implemented. This functionality allows for easy identification of issues, collaboration among developers and the ability to revert to previous versions if needed, promoting better configuration and release management.

domain: Information Systems Operations and Business Resilience

questions.text: Which of the following is the BEST indicator of the effectiveness of backup and restore procedures while restoring data after a disaster?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's storage, backup, and restoration policies and processes

choices.label: A
choices.text: Members of the recovery team were available.
choices.justification: The availability of key personnel does not ensure that backup and restore procedures will work effectively.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recovery time objectives (RTOs) were met.
choices.justification: Effective backup and restore procedures are ensured by meeting recovery time objectives (RTOs), which are defined during the business impact analysis (BIA) stage with input from business process owners.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Inventory of backup media was properly maintained.
choices.justification: The inventory of the backup media is only one element of the successful recovery.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Backup media was completely restored at an alternate site.
choices.justification: The restoration of backup media is a critical success, but only if they were able to be restored within the time frames set by the RTO.

questions.text: The PRIMARY purpose of implementing redundant array of inexpensive disks (RAID) level 1 in a file server is to:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's storage, backup, and restoration policies and processes

choices.label: A
choices.text: achieve performance improvement.
choices.justification: redundant array of inexpensive disks (RAID) level 1 does not improve performance. It writes the data to two separate disk drives.

choices.label: B
choices.text: provide user authentication.
choices.justification: RAID level 1 has no relevance to authentication.

choices.label: C
choices.text: ensure availability of data.
choices.justification: RAID level 1 provides disk mirroring. Data written to one disk are also written to another disk. Users in the network access data on the first disk; if disk one fails, the second disk takes over. This redundancy ensures the availability of data.

choices.label: D
choices.text: ensure the confidentiality of data.
choices.justification: RAID level 1 does nothing to provide for data confidentiality.

questions.text: It is MOST appropriate to implement an incremental backup scheme when:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's storage, backup, and restoration policies and processes

choices.label: A
choices.text: there is limited recovery time for critical data.
choices.justification: A full backup or differential backup is preferred in this situation.

choices.label: B
choices.text: online disk-based media are preferred.
choices.justification: Incremental backup could be used irrespective of the media adopted.

choices.label: C
choices.text: there is limited media capacity.
choices.justification: In an incremental backup, after the full backup, only the files that have changed are backed up, thus minimizing media storage.

choices.label: D
choices.text: a random selection of backup sets is required.
choices.justification: A random selection of backup sets may not be possible with an incremental backup scheme because only fragments of the data are backed up on a daily basis.

questions.text: Which of the following database controls ensure that the integrity of transactions is maintained in an online transaction processing system’s database?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's storage, backup, and restoration policies and processes

choices.label: A
choices.text: Authentication controls
choices.justification: Authentication controls ensure that only authorized personnel can make changes but do not ensure the integrity of the changes.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Data normalization controls
choices.justification: Data normalization is not used to protect the integrity of online transactions.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Read/write access log controls
choices.justification: Log controls are a detective control but do not ensure the integrity of the data in the database.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Commitment and rollback controls
choices.justification: Commitment and rollback controls are directly relevant to integrity. These controls ensure that database operations that form a logical transaction unit will be completed entirely or not at all (i.e., if, for some reason, a transaction cannot be fully completed, then incomplete inserts/updates/deletes are rolled back so that the database returns to its pretransition state).

questions.text: In addition to the backup considerations for all systems, which of the following is an important consideration in providing backup for online systems?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's storage, backup, and restoration policies and processes

choices.label: A
choices.text: Maintaining system software parameters
choices.justification: Maintaining system software parameters is important for all systems, not just online systems.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Ensuring periodic dumps of transaction logs
choices.justification: Regular transaction log dumps are vital to preserving timely data in online systems, acting as a crucial backup to prevent data loss. Given the high activity volume in online systems, the traditional backup method may not be feasible.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Ensuring grandfather-father-son file backups
choices.justification: Having generations of backups is a good practice for all systems.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Maintaining important data at an offsite location
choices.justification: All backups should consider offsite storage at a location that is accessible but not likely to be affected by the same disaster.

questions.text: Which of the following inputs would PRIMARILY help in designing the data backup strategy in case of potential natural disasters?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's storage, backup, and restoration policies and processes

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recovery point objective (RPO)
choices.justification: The recovery point objective (RPO) determines acceptable data loss and the earliest recovery point in time, quantifying the acceptable data loss in case of interruption for designing backup strategies.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Volume of data to be backed up
choices.justification: Although the amount of data to be stored is critical in terms of planning for adequate capacity, the speed of recovery required by the business is the most important factor.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Available data backup technologies
choices.justification: Although a solid understanding of the capabilities of all types of advanced data backup technologies is necessary, without the knowledge of the RPO, one cannot design a backup strategy using these technologies.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Recovery time objective (RTO)
choices.justification: The recovery time objective (RTO) is the amount of time allowed for the recovery of a business function or resource after a disaster occurs. This helps in designing disaster site options, but not the data backup strategy in the case of impacting disasters.

questions.text: Which of the following backup techniques is the MOST appropriate when an organization requires extremely granular data restore points, as defined in the recovery point objective (RPO)?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's storage, backup, and restoration policies and processes

choices.label: A
choices.text: Virtual tape libraries
choices.justification: Virtual tape libraries require time to complete the backup, while continuous data backup happens online (in real time).

choices.label: B
choices.text: Disk-based snapshots
choices.justification: Disk-based snapshots require time to complete the backup and lose some data between the times of the backup and the failure, while continuous data backup happens online (in real time).

choices.label: C
choices.text: Continuous data backup
choices.justification: Recovery point objective (RPO) is based on the acceptable data loss in the case of a disruption. In this scenario, the organization needs a short RPO and continuous data backup is the best option.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Disk-to-tape backup
choices.justification: Disk-to-tape backup requires time to complete the backup, while continuous data backup happens online (in real time).

questions.text: IT management has decided to install a level 1 redundant array of inexpensive disks (RAID) system in all servers to compensate for the elimination of offsite backups. The information systems (IS) auditor should recommend:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's storage, backup, and restoration policies and processes

choices.label: A
choices.text: upgrading to a level 5 redundant array of inexpensive disks (RAID).
choices.justification: Upgrading to level 5 RAID does not address the problem of catastrophic failure of the data center housing all the data.

choices.label: B
choices.text: increasing the frequency of onsite backups.
choices.justification: Increasing the frequency of onsite backups is not relevant to RAID 1 because all data are being mirrored already.

choices.label: C
choices.text: reinstating the offsite backups.
choices.justification: A RAID system, at any level, does not protect against a natural disaster. The problem will not be alleviated without offsite backups.

choices.label: D
choices.text: establishing a cold site in a secure location.
choices.justification: A cold site is an offsite recovery location but does not provide for data recovery, because a cold site is not used to store data.

questions.text: A review of wide area network (WAN) usage discovers that traffic on one communication line between sites, synchronously linking the master and standby database, peaks at 96 percent of the line capacity. An information systems (IS) auditor should conclude that:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's data governance program

choices.label: A
choices.text: analysis is required to determine if a pattern emerges that results in a service loss for a short period of time.
choices.justification: The peak at 96 percent can be the result of a one-off incident (e.g., a user downloading a large amount of data); therefore, analysis to establish whether this is a regular pattern and what causes this behavior should be carried out before expenditure on a larger line capacity is recommended.

choices.label: B
choices.text: WAN capacity is adequate for the maximum traffic demands because saturation has not been reached.
choices.justification: A peak traffic load of 96 percent is approaching a critical level, and the auditor should not assume that capacity is adequate at this time or for the foreseeable future. Further investigation is required.

choices.label: C
choices.text: the line should immediately be replaced by one with a larger capacity to provide approximately 85 percent saturation.
choices.justification: If the peak is established to be a regular occurrence without any other opportunities for mitigation (usage of bandwidth reservation protocol or other types of prioritizing network traffic), the line should be replaced because there is the risk of loss of service as the traffic approaches 100 percent. At this point, further research is required.

choices.label: D
choices.text: users should be instructed to reduce their traffic demands or distribute them across all service hours to flatten bandwidth consumption.
choices.justification: If the peak traffic load is a rare one-off occurrence or if traffic can be reengineered to transfer at other time frames, then user education may be an option. Further investigation will be required.

questions.text: An organization with a limited budget has a recovery time objective (RTO) of 72 hours and a recovery point objective (RPO) of 24 hours. Which of the following would BEST meet the requirements of the business?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's storage, backup, and restoration policies and processes

choices.label: A
choices.text: Hot site
choices.justification: Although a hot site enables the business to meet its recovery point objective (RPO) and recovery time objective (RTO), the cost to maintain a hot site is more than the cost to maintain a warm site, which can also meet the objectives.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Cold site
choices.justification: A cold site, although providing basic infrastructure, lacks the required hardware to meet the business objectives.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Mirrored site
choices.justification: A mirrored site provides fully redundant facilities with real-time data replication. It can meet the business objectives, but it is not as cost-effective a solution as a warm site.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Warm site
choices.justification: A warm site is a suitable solution because it provides essential infrastructure and most of the required IT equipment at a reasonable cost. Additional equipment can be obtained through vendor agreements within days.

questions.text: An information systems (IS) auditor is reviewing the enterprise’s IT environment and identified that the IT operations team implemented an enhanced level of security over data. Which of the following object-oriented technology characteristic is MOST likely be implemented by the IT operations team?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's data governance program

choices.label: A
choices.text: Inheritance
choices.justification: In object-oriented systems, an object is called by another module and inherits its data from the calling module. This does not affect security.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Dynamic warehousing
choices.justification: Dynamic warehousing is not related to the security of object-oriented technology.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Encapsulation
choices.justification: Encapsulation is a property of objects, and it prevents accessing either properties or methods that have not been previously defined as public. This means that any implementation of the behavior of an object is not accessible. An object defines a communication interface with the exterior and only that which belongs to that interface can be accessed.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Polymorphism
choices.justification: Polymorphism is the principle of creating different objects that will behave differently depending on the input. This is not a security feature.

questions.text: Which of the following processes will be MOST effective in reducing the risk that unauthorized software on a backup server is distributed to the production server?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's storage, backup, and restoration policies and processes

choices.label: A
choices.text: Manually copy files to accomplish replication.
choices.justification: Even if replication is conducted manually with due care, there remains a risk to copying unauthorized software from one server to another.

choices.label: B
choices.text: changes in the software version control system.
choices.justification: It is common practice for software changes to be tracked and controlled using version control software. An information systems (IS) auditor should review reports or logs from this system to identify the software that is promoted to production. Only moving the versions on the version control system program will prevent the transfer of development or earlier versions.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Ensure that developers do not have access to the backup server.
choices.justification: If unauthorized code was introduced onto the backup server by developers, controls on the production server and the software version control system should mitigate this risk.

choices.label: D
choices.text: the access control log of the backup server.
choices.justification: of the access log will identify staff access or the operations performed; however, it may not provide enough information to detect the release of unauthorized software.

questions.text: Data flow diagrams are used by information systems (IS) auditors to:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's data governance program

choices.label: A
choices.text: identify key controls.
choices.justification: Identifying key controls is not the focus of data flow diagrams. The focus is the flow of data.

choices.label: B
choices.text: highlight high-level data definitions.
choices.justification: A data dictionary may be used to document data definitions, but the data flow diagram is used to document how data move through a process.

choices.label: C
choices.text: graphically summarize data paths and storage.
choices.justification: Data flow diagrams are used as aids to graph or chart data flow and storage. They trace data from their origination to their destination, highlighting the paths and storage of data.

choices.label: D
choices.text: portray step-by-step details of data generation.
choices.justification: The purpose of a data flow diagram is to track the movement of data through a process and is not primarily to document or indicate how data are generated.

questions.text: Which of the following BEST mitigates the risk of backup media containing irreplaceable information being lost or stolen while in transit?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: Ensure that media are encrypted.
choices.justification: Although strong encryption protects against disclosure, it will not mitigate the loss of irreplaceable data.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Maintain a duplicate copy.
choices.justification: Sensitive data should always be fully backed up before being transmitted or moved. Backups of sensitive information should be treated with the same control considerations as the actual data.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Maintain chain of custody.
choices.justification: Chain of custody is an important control, but it will not mitigate a loss if a locked area is broken into and media removed or if media are lost while in an individual’s custody.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Ensure that personnel are bonded.
choices.justification: Bonded security, although good for preventing theft, will not protect against accidental loss or destruction.

questions.text: Applying a retention date on a file will ensure that:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's storage, backup, and restoration policies and processes

choices.label: A
choices.text: data cannot be read until the date is set.
choices.justification: The retention date does not affect the ability to read the file.

choices.label: B
choices.text: data will not be deleted before that date.
choices.justification: A retention date ensures that a file cannot be overwritten or deleted before that date has passed.

choices.label: C
choices.text: backup copies are not retained after that date.
choices.justification: Backup copies would be expected to have a different retention date and, therefore, may be retained after the file has been overwritten.

choices.label: D
choices.text: datasets having the same name are differentiated.
choices.justification: The creation date, not the retention date, differentiates files with the same name.

questions.text: An information systems (IS) auditor finds that the data warehouse query performance decreases significantly at certain times of the day. Which of the following controls are MOST relevant for the IS auditor to review?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's storage, backup, and restoration policies and processes

choices.label: A
choices.text: Permanent table-space allocation
choices.justification: Table-space allocation does not affect performance at different times of the day.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Commitment and rollback controls
choices.justification: Commitment and rollback only apply to errors or failures and will not affect performance at different times of the day.

choices.label: C
choices.text: User spool and database limit controls
choices.justification: User spool limits restrict the space available for running user queries. This prevents poorly formed queries from consuming excessive system resources and impacting general query performance. Limiting the space available to users in their own databases prevents them from building excessively large tables. This helps to control space utilization, which itself acts to help performance by maintaining a buffer between the actual data volume stored and the physical device capacity. Additionally, it prevents users from consuming excessive resources in ad hoc table builds (as opposed to scheduled production loads that often can run overnight and are optimized for performance purposes). In a data warehouse, because online transactions are not being run, commitment and rollback does not have an impact on performance.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Read/write access log controls
choices.justification: Read/write access log controls do not affect performance at different times of the day.

questions.text: Which of the following is the MOST efficient strategy for the backup of large quantities of mission-critical data when the systems need to be online to take sales orders 24 hours a day?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's storage, backup, and restoration policies and processes

choices.label: A
choices.text: Implementing a fault-tolerant disk-to-disk backup solution.
choices.justification: Disk-to-disk backup involves writing the primary backup to disk instead of tape. This allows for later copying, cloning or migration to tape. It ensures minimal system performance impact, enables fast backups of large data volumes and offers immediate transfer to an alternate disk set in case of failure.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Making a full backup weekly and an incremental backup nightly.
choices.justification: Although a backup strategy is valid, because many computer systems must be taken offline so that backups can be performed, there is the need to create a backup window, typically during each night. This does not enable the system to be available 24/7. For a system that must remain online at all times, the only feasible way to back up the data is to either duplicate the data to a server that gets backed up to magnetic media or deploy a disk-to-disk solution, which is effectively the same thing.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Creating a duplicate storage area network (SAN) and replicating the data to a second SAN.
choices.justification: Although creating a duplicate storage area network (SAN) and replicating the data to a second SAN provides some redundancy and data protection, this is not really a backup solution. If the two systems are at the same site, there is a risk that an incident such as a fire or flood in the data center can lead to data loss.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Creating identical server and storage infrastructure at a hot site.
choices.justification: Although creating an identical server and storage infrastructure at a hot site provides a great deal of redundancy and availability to enable the system to stay operational, it does not address the need for long-term data storage. There is still the need to create an efficient method of backing up data.

questions.text: In the event of a data center disaster, which of the following is the MOST appropriate strategy to enable a complete recovery of a critical database?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's storage, backup, and restoration policies and processes

choices.label: A
choices.text: Daily data backup to a remote site
choices.justification: Daily backup recovery can result in the loss of data from a day’s work of data.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Real-time replication to a remote site
choices.justification: With real-time replication to a remote site, data are updated simultaneously in two separate locations; therefore, a disaster in one site does not damage the information located in the remote site. This assumes that both sites are not affected by the same disaster.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Hard disk mirroring to a local server
choices.justification: Hard disk mirroring to a local server takes place in the same data center and can possibly be affected by the same disaster.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Real-time data backup to the local storage area network
choices.justification: Real-time data backup to the local storage area network takes place in the same data center and can possibly be affected by the same disaster.

questions.text: When auditing the onsite archiving process of emails, the information systems (IS) auditor should pay the MOST attention to which of the following?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's storage, backup, and restoration policies and processes

choices.label: A
choices.text: Existence of a data retention policy
choices.justification: Without a data retention policy that is aligned with the organization’s business and compliance requirements, the email archive may not preserve and reproduce the correct information when required.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Storage capacity of the archiving solution
choices.justification: The storage capacity of the archiving solution is irrelevant if the proper email messages have not been properly preserved and others have been deleted.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Level of user awareness concerning email use
choices.justification: The level of user awareness concerning email use does not directly affect the completeness and accuracy of the archived email.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Support and stability of the archiving solution manufacturer
choices.justification: The support and stability of the archiving solution manufacturer is secondary to the need to ensure a retention policy. Vendor support does not directly affect the completeness and accuracy of the archived email.

questions.text: Which of the following BEST supports 24/7 availability?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's storage, backup, and restoration policies and processes

choices.label: A
choices.text: Daily backup
choices.justification: Daily backup implies that it is reasonable for restoration to take place within a number of hours but not immediately.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Offsite storage
choices.justification: Offsite storage does not, itself, support continuous availability.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Mirroring
choices.justification: Mirroring of critical elements is a tool that facilitates immediate (failover) recoverability.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Periodic testing
choices.justification: Periodic testing of systems does not, itself, support continuous availability.

questions.text: What is the GREATEST risk associated with inadequate management of storage growth in a critical file server?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's storage, backup, and restoration policies and processes

choices.label: A
choices.text: Backup time steadily increases.
choices.justification: Backup time may increase, but that can be managed. The most important issue is the time taken to recover the data.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Backup operational costs significantly increase.
choices.justification: The backup cost issues are not as significant as not meeting the recovery time objective (RTO).

choices.label: C
choices.text: Storage operational costs significantly increase.
choices.justification: The storage cost issues are not as significant as not meeting the RTO.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Server recovery work may not meet the recovery time objective (RTO).
choices.justification: In case of a crash, recovering a server with an extensive amount of data can require a significant amount of time. If the recovery cannot meet the RTO, there will be a discrepancy in IT strategies. It is important to ensure that server restoration can meet the RTO.

questions.text: Which of the following is the MOST important criterion when selecting a location for an offsite storage facility for information systems (IS) backup files? The offsite facility must be:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's storage, backup, and restoration policies and processes

choices.label: A
choices.text: physically separated from the data center and not subject to the same risk.
choices.justification: It is important that there is an offsite storage location for information systems (IS) files and that it is in a location not subject to the same risk as the primary data center.

choices.label: B
choices.text: given the same level of protection as that of the computer data center.
choices.justification: The offsite location may be shared with other organizations and, therefore, have an even higher level of protection than the primary data center.

choices.label: C
choices.text: outsourced to a reliable third party.
choices.justification: An offsite location may be owned by a third party or by the organization itself.

choices.label: D
choices.text: equipped with surveillance capabilities.
choices.justification: Physical protection is important but not as important as not being affected by the same crisis.

questions.text: Which of the following is the MOST important consideration when defining recovery point objectives (RPOs)?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's storage, backup, and restoration policies and processes

choices.label: A
choices.text: Minimum operating requirements
choices.justification: Minimum operating requirements help define recovery strategies.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Acceptable data loss
choices.justification: Recovery point objectives (RPOs) are the level of data loss/reworking an organization is willing to accept.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Mean time between failures
choices.justification: Mean time between failures helps define likelihood of system failure.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Acceptable time for recovery
choices.justification: Recovery time objectives (RTOs) are the acceptable time delay in availability of business operations.

questions.text: Which of the following should an information systems (IS) auditor recommend for the protection of specific sensitive information stored in a data warehouse?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's database management practices

choices.label: A
choices.text: Implement column- and row-level permissions.
choices.justification: Column- and row-level permissions control what information users can access. Column-level security prevents users from seeing one or more attributes on a table. With row-level security, a certain grouping of information on a table is restricted (e.g., if a table held details of employee salaries, then a restriction can be put in place to ensure that, unless specifically authorized, users cannot view the salaries of executive staff). Column- and row-level security can be achieved in a relational database by allowing users to access logical representations of data (views) rather than physical tables. This fine-grained security model is likely to offer the best balance between information protection while still supporting a wide range of analytical and reporting uses.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Enhance user authentication via strong passwords.
choices.justification: Enhancing user authentication via strong passwords is a security control that should apply to all users of the data warehouse and does not specifically address protection of specific sensitive data.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Organize the data warehouse into subject matter-specific databases.
choices.justification: Organizing a data warehouse into subject-specific databases is a potentially useful practice but, alone, does not adequately protect sensitive data. Database-level security is normally too coarse of a level to efficiently and effectively protect information. For example, one database may hold information that needs to be restricted, such as employee salary and customer profitability details, while other information, such as employee department, may need to be legitimately accessed by a large number of users. Organizing the data warehouse into subject-matter-specific databases is similar to user access in that this control should generally apply. Extra attention can be devoted to reviewing access to tables with sensitive data, but this control is not sufficient without strong preventive controls at the column and row level.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Log user access to the data warehouse.
choices.justification: Logging user access is important, but it is only a detective control that does not provide adequate protection to sensitive information.

domain: Information Systems Operations and Business Resilience

questions.text: An information systems (IS) auditor conducting a review of software usage and licensing discovers that numerous PCs contain unauthorized software. Which of the following actions should the IS auditor take?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's policies and practices related to asset life cycle management

choices.label: A
choices.text: Delete all copies of the unauthorized software.
choices.justification: An information systems (IS) auditor should not assume the role of the enforcing officer and take on any personal involvement in removing the unauthorized software.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recommend an automated process to monitor for compliance with software licensing.
choices.justification: This would detect compliance with software licensing; however, an automated solution might not be the best option in all cases.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Report the use of the unauthorized software and the need to prevent recurrence.
choices.justification: The use of unauthorized or illegal software should be prohibited by an organization. An IS auditor must convince the user and management of the risk and the need to eliminate the risk. For example, software piracy can result in exposure and severe fines.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Warn the end users about the risk of using illegal software.
choices.justification: Auditors must report material findings to management for action. Informing the users of risk is not the primary responsibility of the IS auditor.

questions.text: Which of the following is a network diagnostic tool that monitors and records network information?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's information security program

choices.label: A
choices.text: Online monitor
choices.justification: Online monitors measure telecommunication transmissions and determine whether transmissions were accurate and complete.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Downtime report
choices.justification: Downtime reports track the availability of telecommunication lines and circuits.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Help desk report
choices.justification: Help desk reports are prepared by the help desk, which is staffed or supported by information systems (IS) technical support personnel trained to handle problems occurring during the course of IS operations.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Protocol analyzer
choices.justification: Protocol analyzers are network diagnostic tools that monitor and record network information from packets traveling in the link to which the analyzer is attached.

questions.text: When reviewing the desktop software compliance of an organization, the information systems (IS) auditor should be MOST concerned if the installed software:
questions.taskStatement: Plan an audit to determine whether information systems are protected, controlled, and provide value to the organization

choices.label: A
choices.text: is installed, but not documented in the IT department records.
choices.justification: All software, including licenses, should be documented in IT department records, but this is not as serious as the violation of policy in installing unapproved software.

choices.label: B
choices.text: is being used by users not properly trained in its use.
choices.justification: Discovering that users have not been formally trained in the use of a software product is common, and, although not ideal, most software includes help files and other tips that can assist in learning how to use the software effectively.

choices.label: C
choices.text: is not listed in the approved software standards document.
choices.justification: Installing software not allowed by policy is a serious violation and can put the organization at security, legal and financial risk. Any software that is allowed should be part of a standard software list. This is the first thing to review because this would also indicate compliance with policies.

choices.label: D
choices.text: has a license that will expire in the next 15 days.
choices.justification: A software license that is about to expire is not a risk if there is a process in place to renew it.

questions.text: An information systems (IS) auditor discovers that some users installed personal software on their PCs. This is not explicitly forbidden by the security policy. The BEST approach for an IS auditor is to recommend that the:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's management of IT policies and practices, including compliance with legal and regulatory requirements

choices.label: A
choices.text: IT department implement control mechanisms to prevent unauthorized software installation.
choices.justification: An information systems (IS) auditor’s obligation is to report on observations noted and make the best recommendation, which is to address the situation through policy. The IT department cannot implement controls in the absence of the authority provided through policy.

choices.label: B
choices.text: security policy be updated to include the specific language regarding unauthorized software.
choices.justification: Lack of specific language addressing unauthorized software in the acceptable use policy is a weakness in administrative controls. The policy should be reviewed and updated to address the issue—and provide authority for the IT department to implement technical controls.

choices.label: C
choices.text: IT department prohibit the download of unauthorized software.
choices.justification: Preventing downloads of unauthorized software is not the complete solution. Unauthorized software can be also introduced through compact disks (CDs) and USB drives.

choices.label: D
choices.text: users obtain approval from an IS manager before installing nonstandard software.
choices.justification: Requiring approval from the IS manager before installation of the nonstandard software is an exception handling control. It would not be effective unless a preventive control to prohibit user installation of unauthorized software is established first.

questions.text: During an assessment of software development practices, an information systems (IS) auditor finds that open-source software components were used in an application designed for a client. What is the GREATEST concern that the auditor has about the use of open-source software?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate controls at all stages of the information systems development life cycle

choices.label: A
choices.text: The client did not pay for the open-source software components.
choices.justification: A major benefit of using open-source software is that it is free. The client is not required to pay for the open-source software components; however, both the developing organization and the client should be concerned about the licensing terms and conditions of the open source software components that are being used.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The organization and client must comply with open-source software license terms.
choices.justification: There are many types of open-source software licenses and each has different terms and conditions. Some open-source software licensing allows use of the open-source software component freely but requires that the completed software product must also allow the same rights. This is known as viral licensing, and if the development organization is not careful, its products can violate licensing terms by selling the product for profit. The information systems (IS) auditor should be most concerned with open-source software licensing compliance to avoid unintended intellectual property risk or legal consequences.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Open-source software has security vulnerabilities.
choices.justification: Open-source software, just like any software code, should be tested for security flaws and should be part of the normal system development life cycle (SDLC) process. This is not more of a concern than licensing compliance.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Open-source software is unreliable for commercial use.
choices.justification: Open-source software does not inherently lack quality. Like any software code, it should be tested for reliability and should be part of the normal SDLC process. This is not more of a concern than licensing compliance.

questions.text: Although management has stated otherwise, an information systems (IS) auditor has reasons to believe that the organization is using software that is not licensed. In this situation, the IS auditor should FIRST:
questions.taskStatement: Determine whether the organization has defined ownership of IT risk, controls, and standards

choices.label: A
choices.text: include the statement from management in the audit report.
choices.justification: The statement from management may be included in the audit report, but the auditor should independently validate the statements made by management to ensure completeness and accuracy.

choices.label: B
choices.text: verify that the software is in use through testing.
choices.justification: When there is an indication that an organization might be using unlicensed software, the information systems (IS) auditor should obtain sufficient evidence before including it in the report.

choices.label: C
choices.text: include the item in the audit report.
choices.justification: With respect to this matter, representations obtained from management cannot be independently verified.

choices.label: D
choices.text: discuss the issue with senior management because it could have a negative impact on the organization.
choices.justification: If the organization is using software that is not licensed, the IS auditor must include this in the report to maintain objectivity and independence, but the IS auditor should verify that this is the case before presenting it to senior management.

questions.text: Which of the following is a MAJOR concern during a review of help desk activities?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate whether the business cases related to information systems meet business objectives

choices.label: A
choices.text: The help desk team could not resolve certain calls.
choices.justification: Although this is of concern, it should be expected. A problem escalation procedure should be developed to handle such scenarios.

choices.label: B
choices.text: A dedicated line is not assigned to the help desk team.
choices.justification: Ideally, a help desk team should have dedicated lines, but this exception is not as serious as the technical team unilaterally closing an incident.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Resolved incidents are closed without reference to end users.
choices.justification: The help desk function is a service-oriented unit. The end users must be advised before an incident can be regarded as closed.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The help desk instant messaging has been down for more than six months.
choices.justification: Instant messaging is an add-on to improve the effectiveness of the help desk team. Its absence cannot be seen as a major concern if calls can still be made.

domain: Information Systems Operations and Business Resilience

questions.text: The MAIN reason for requiring that all computer clocks across an enterprise are synchronized is to:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's ability to continue business operations

choices.label: A
choices.text: prevent omission or duplication of transactions.
choices.justification: The possibility of omission or duplication of transactions will not happen due to lack of clock synchronization.

choices.label: B
choices.text: ensure smooth data transition from client machines to servers.
choices.justification: Data transfer has nothing to do with the time stamp.

choices.label: C
choices.text: ensure that email messages have accurate time stamps.
choices.justification: Although the time stamp on an email may not be accurate, this is not a significant issue.

choices.label: D
choices.text: support the incident investigation process.
choices.justification: During an investigation of incidents, audit logs are used as evidence, and the time stamp information in them is useful. If the clocks are not synchronized, investigations will be more difficult, because a timeline of events occurring on different systems might not be easily established.

questions.text: Which of the following controls is MOST effective in ensuring that production source code and object code are synchronized?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the readiness of information systems for implementation and migration into production

choices.label: A
choices.text: Release-to-release source and object comparison reports
choices.justification: Using version control software and comparing source and object code is a good practice but may not detect a problem where the source code is a different version than the object code.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Library control software restricting changes to source code
choices.justification: All production libraries should be protected with access controls, and this may protect source code from tampering. However, this will not ensure that source and object codes are based on the same version.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Restricted access to source code and object code
choices.justification: Protecting all source and object code—even in development—s a good practice. However, this will not ensure the synchronization of source and object code.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Date and time-stamp reviews of source and object code
choices.justification: Date and time-stamp reviews of source and object code ensures that the source code, which has been compiled, matches the production object code. This is the most effective way to ensure that the approved production source code is compiled and is the one being used.

questions.text: Which of the following reports should an information systems (IS) auditor use to check compliance with a service level agreement’s requirement for uptime?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the monitoring and reporting of IT key performance indicators (KPIs) and IT key risk indicators (KRIs)

choices.label: A
choices.text: Utilization reports
choices.justification: Utilization reports document the use of computer equipment, and can be used by management to predict how, where and/or when resources are required.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Hardware error reports
choices.justification: Hardware error reports provide information to aid in detecting hardware failures and initiating corrective action. These error reports may not indicate actual system uptime.

choices.label: C
choices.text: System logs
choices.justification: System logs are used for recording system’s activities. They may not indicate availability.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Availability reports
choices.justification: information systems (IS) inactivity, such as downtime, is addressed by availability reports. These reports provide the time periods during which the computer was available for utilization by users or other processes.

questions.text: An information systems (IS) auditor observed that multiple applications are hosted on the same server. The recovery time objective (RTO) for the server is:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the monitoring and reporting of IT key performance indicators (KPIs) and IT key risk indicators (KRIs)

choices.label: A
choices.text: based on the application with the longest RTO.
choices.justification: The longest recovery time objective (RTO) is determined for noncritical applications, which does not help in meeting the objectives for critical systems.

choices.label: B
choices.text: based on the application with the shortest RTO.
choices.justification: When several applications are hosted on a server, the server’s RTO must be determined by taking the RTO of the most critical application, which is the shortest RTO.

choices.label: C
choices.text: based on the mean of each application’s RTO.
choices.justification: The mean value is higher than the RTO for a critical application.

choices.label: D
choices.text: independent of the RTO and based on the criticality of the application.
choices.justification: Critical applications usually have the shortest RTOs. The RTO of the server cannot be independent of the application RTO.

questions.text: Which recovery strategy is MOST appropriate for a business with multiple offices within a region and a limited recovery budget?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's ability to continue business operations

choices.label: A
choices.text: Hot site maintained by the business
choices.justification: A hot site maintained by the business would be costly but would provide a high degree of confidence.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Commercial cold site
choices.justification: Multiple cold sites leased for the multiple offices would lead to an ineffective solution with poor availability.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Reciprocal arrangement between its offices
choices.justification: A reciprocal arrangement among its offices would be most appropriate for a business with many offices within a region. Each office can be designated as a recovery site for some other office. This would be the least expensive approach and would provide an acceptable level of confidence.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Third-party hot site
choices.justification: A traditional hot site provides a third-party facility for recovery. This would be a costly approach providing a high degree of confidence.

questions.text: An information systems (IS) auditor is reviewing an organization’s recovery from a disaster in which not all the critical data needed to resume business operations were retained. Which of the following was incorrectly defined?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's ability to continue business operations

choices.label: A
choices.text: Interruption window
choices.justification: The interruption window is defined as the amount of time during which the organization is unable to maintain operations from the point of failure to the time that the critical services/applications are restored.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recovery time objective
choices.justification: The recovery time objective is determined based on the acceptable downtime in the case of a disruption of operations.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Service delivery objective
choices.justification: The service delivery objective (SDO) is directly related to the business needs. SDO is the level of services to be reached during the alternate process mode until the normal situation is restored.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Recovery point objective
choices.justification: The recovery point objective (RPO) is determined based on the acceptable data loss in the case of a disruption of operations. RPO defines the point in time from which it is necessary to recover the data and quantifies, in terms of time, the permissible amount of data loss in the case of interruption.

questions.text: In evaluating programmed controls over password management, which of the following is the information systems (IS) auditor MOST likely to rely on?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate controls at all stages of the information systems development life cycle

choices.label: A
choices.text: Size check
choices.justification: A size check is useful because passwords should have a minimum length, but it is not as strong of a control as validity.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Hash total
choices.justification: Passwords are not typically entered in a batch mode, so a hash total would not be effective. More important, a system should not accept incorrect values of a password, so a hash total as a control will not indicate any weak passwords, errors or omissions.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Validity check
