questions.text: A web server is attacked and compromised. Enterprise policy states that incident response should balance containment of an attack with retaining freedom for later legal action against an attacker. Under the circumstances, which of the following should be performed FIRST?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's problem and incident management program

choices.label: A
choices.text: Dump the volatile storage data to a disk.
choices.justification: Dumping the volatile storage data to a disk may be used at the investigation stage but does not contain an attack in progress.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Run the server in a fail-safe mode.
choices.justification: To run the server in a fail-safe mode, the server needs to be shut down.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Disconnect the web server from the network.
choices.justification: The first action is to disconnect the web server from the network to secure the device for investigation, contain the damage and prevent more actions by the attacker.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Shut down the web server.
choices.justification: Shutting down the server can potentially erase information that might be needed for a forensic investigation or to develop a strategy to prevent future similar attacks.

questions.text: Which of the following should an incident response team address FIRST after a major incident in an information processing facility (IPF)?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's problem and incident management program

choices.label: A
choices.text: Restoration at the facility
choices.justification: Restoration ensures that the affected systems or services are restored to a condition specified in the restore point objective. This action will be possible only after containment of the damage.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Documentation of the facility
choices.justification: Documentation of the facility should be prepared to inform management of the incident; however, damage must be contained first.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Containment at the facility
choices.justification: The first priority (after addressing life safety) is the containment of the incident at the facility so that spread of the damage is minimized. The incident team must gain control of the situation.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Monitoring of the facility
choices.justification: Monitoring of the facility is important, although containment must take priority to avoid spread of the damage.

questions.text: The computer security incident response team of an enterprise disseminates detailed descriptions of recent threats. An information systems (IS) auditor’s GREATEST concern should be that the users may:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's problem and incident management program

choices.label: A
choices.text: use this information to launch attacks.
choices.justification: The enterprise’s computer security incident response team (CSIRT) should disseminate recent threats, security guidelines and security updates to the users to assist them in understanding the security risk of errors and omissions. However, this introduces the risk that the users may use this information to launch attacks, directly or indirectly. An information systems (IS) auditor should ensure that the CSIRT is actively involved with users to assist them in mitigation of risk arising from security failures and to prevent additional security incidents resulting from the same threat.

choices.label: B
choices.text: forward the security alert.
choices.justification: Forwarding the security alert is not harmful to the enterprise.

choices.label: C
choices.text: implement individual solutions.
choices.justification: Implementing individual solutions is unlikely and inefficient, but not a serious risk.

choices.label: D
choices.text: fail to understand the threat.
choices.justification: Users failing to understand the threat is not a serious concern.

questions.text: Email traffic from the Internet is routed via firewall-1 to the mail gateway. Mail is routed from the mail gateway, via firewall-2, to the mail recipients in the internal network. Other traffic is not allowed. For example, the firewalls do not allow direct traffic from the Internet to the internal network. The intrusion detection system (IDS) detects traffic for the internal network that did not originate from the mail gateway. The FIRST action triggered by the IDS should be to:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's threat and vulnerability management program

choices.label: A
choices.text: alert the appropriate staff.
choices.justification: The first action taken by an intrusion detection system (IDS) is to create a log entry and then alert the appropriate staff.

choices.label: B
choices.text: create an entry in the log.
choices.justification: Creating an entry in the log is the first step taken by a network IDS. The IDS may also be configured to send an alert to the administrator, send a note to the firewall and may even be configured to record the suspicious packet.

choices.label: C
choices.text: close firewall-2.
choices.justification: Traffic for the internal network that did not originate from the mail gateway is a sign that firewall-1 is not functioning properly. This may have been caused by an attack from a hacker. After the IDS has logged the suspicious traffic, it may signal firewall-2 to close, thus preventing damage to the internal network. After closing firewall-2, the malfunctioning of firewall-1 can be investigated. The IDS should trigger the closing of firewall-2 either automatically or by manual intervention. Between the detection by the IDS and a response from the system administrator, valuable time can be lost, in which a hacker can also compromise firewall-2.

choices.label: D
choices.text: close firewall-1.
choices.justification: The IDS will usually only protect the internal network by closing firewall-2 and will not close the externally facing firewall-1.

questions.text: Which of the following is an indicator of the effectiveness of a computer security incident response team?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's problem and incident management program

choices.label: A
choices.text: Financial impact per security incident
choices.justification: The most important indicator is the financial impact per security incident. It may not be possible to prevent incidents entirely, but the team should be able to limit the cost of incidents through a combination of effective prevention, detection and response.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Number of security vulnerabilities that were patched
choices.justification: Patching of security vulnerabilities is important but not a direct responsibility of the computer security incident response team (CSIRT).

choices.label: C
choices.text: Percentage of business applications that are being protected
choices.justification: The CSIRT is not responsible for the protection of systems. That is the responsibility of the security team.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Number of successful penetration tests
choices.justification: The number of penetration tests measures the effectiveness of the security team and the patch management process, but not the effectiveness of the CSIRT.

questions.text: Which of the following specifically addresses how to deal with unauthorized activity on the enterprise’s IT systems and how to recover from an attack?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's problem and incident management program

choices.label: A
choices.text: Incident response plan (IRP)
choices.justification: The incident response plan (IRP) determines the information security responses to incidents such as cyberattacks on systems and/or networks. This plan establishes procedures to enable security personnel to identify, mitigate and recover from malicious computer incidents, such as unauthorized access to a system or data, denial-of-service or unauthorized changes to system hardware or software.

choices.label: B
choices.text: IT contingency plan
choices.justification: The IT contingency plan addresses IT system disruptions and establishes procedures for recovering from a major application or general support system failure. The contingency plan deals with ways to recover from an unexpected failure, but it does not address the identification or prevention of cyberattacks.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Business continuity plan (BCP)
choices.justification: The business continuity plan (BCP) addresses business processes and provides procedures for sustaining essential business operations while recovering from a significant disruption. Although a cyberattack can be severe enough to require use of the BCP, the IRP would be used to determine which actions should be taken—both to stop the attack and to resume normal operations after the attack.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Continuity of operations plan
choices.justification: The continuity of operations plan addresses the subset of enterprise missions that are deemed most critical and contains procedures to sustain these functions at an alternate site for a short time period.

questions.text: An information systems (IS) auditor suspects an incident is occurring while an audit is being performed on a financial system. What should the IS auditor do FIRST?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's problem and incident management program

choices.label: A
choices.text: Request that the system be shut down to preserve evidence.
choices.justification: The information systems (IS) auditor should follow the incident response process of the organization. The auditor is not authorized to shut the system down.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Report the incident to management.
choices.justification: Reporting the suspected incident to management will help initiate the incident response process, which is the most appropriate action. Management is responsible for making decisions regarding the appropriate response. It is not the IS auditor’s role to respond to incidents during an audit.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Ask for immediate suspension of the suspect accounts.
choices.justification: The IS auditor is not authorized to lead the investigation or to suspend user accounts. The auditor should report the incident to management.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Investigate the source and nature of the incident.
choices.justification: Management is responsible for setting up and following an incident management plan; that is not the responsibility of the IS auditor.

category: Security Monitoring Logs, Tools, and Techniques

questions.text: Which of the following controls will MOST effectively detect the presence of bursts of errors in network transmissions?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's information security program

choices.label: A
choices.text: Parity check
choices.justification: Parity check (known as vertical redundancy check) also involves adding a bit (known as the parity bit) to each character during transmission. In this case, where there is a presence of bursts of errors (i.e., impulsing noise during high transmission rates), it has a reliability of approximately 50 percent. At higher transmission rates, this limitation is significant.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Echo check
choices.justification: Echo checks detect line errors by retransmitting data to the sending device for comparison with the original transmission.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Block sum check
choices.justification: A block sum check is a form of parity checking and has a low level of reliability.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Cyclic redundancy check
choices.justification: The cyclic redundancy check (CRC) can check for a block of transmitted data. The workstations generate the CRC and transmit it with the data. The receiving workstation computes a CRC and compares it to the transmitted CRC. If both of them are equal, then the block is assumed error free. In this case (such as, in parity error or echo check), multiple errors can be detected. In general, CRC can detect all single-bit and double-bit errors.

questions.text: An information systems (IS) auditor reviewing the implementation of an intrusion detection system (IDS) should be MOST concerned if:
questions.taskStatement: Utilize technical security testing to identify potential vulnerabilities

choices.label: A
choices.text: IDS sensors are placed outside of the firewall.
choices.justification: An organization can place sensors outside of the firewall to detect attacks. These sensors are placed in highly sensitive areas and on extranets.

choices.label: B
choices.text: a behavior-based IDS is causing many false alarms.
choices.justification: An excessive number of false alarms from a behavior-based intrusion detection system (IDS) indicates that additional tuning is needed. False positives cannot be eliminated entirely, but ignoring this warning sign may negate the value of the system by causing those responsible for monitoring its warnings to become convinced that anything reported is false.

choices.label: C
choices.text: a signature-based IDS is weak against new types of attacks.
choices.justification: Being weak against new types of attacks is expected from a signature-based IDS because it can only recognize attacks that have been previously identified.

choices.label: D
choices.text: the IDS is used to detect encrypted traffic.
choices.justification: An IDS cannot detect attacks within encrypted traffic, but there may be good reason to detect the presence of encrypted traffic, such as when a next-generation firewall is configured to terminate encrypted connections at the perimeter. In such cases, detecting encrypted packets flowing past the firewall can indicate improper configuration or even a compromise of the firewall itself.

questions.text: Security administration procedures require read-only access to:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's log management program

choices.label: A
choices.text: access control tables.
choices.justification: Security administration procedures require write access to access control tables to manage and update the privileges according to authorized business requirements.

choices.label: B
choices.text: security log files.
choices.justification: Security administration procedures require read-only access to security log files to ensure that, once generated, the logs are not modified. Logs provide evidence and track suspicious transactions and activities.

choices.label: C
choices.text: logging options.
choices.justification: Logging options require write access to allow the administrator to update the way the transactions and user activities are monitored, captured, stored, processed and reported.

choices.label: D
choices.text: user profiles.
choices.justification: The security administrator is often responsible for user-facing issues, such as managing user roles, profiles and settings. This requires the administrator to have more than read-only access.

questions.text: Which of the following is the MOST significant concern related to using artificial intelligence (AI) in automated decision-making systems?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate potential opportunities and risks associated with emerging technologies, regulations, and industry practices

choices.label: A
choices.text: artificial intelligence (AI) systems may lack transparency and explainability.
choices.justification: One of the significant concerns related to using artificial intelligence (AI) in automated decision-making systems is the lack of transparency and explainability. AI algorithms, especially those based on deep learning or neural networks, can be complex and difficult to interpret. This lack of transparency can make understanding how AI systems arrive at specific decisions or recommendations challenging, which may raise concerns about bias, ethics and accountability.

choices.label: B
choices.text: AI algorithms have limited accuracy and reliability.
choices.justification: Although AI algorithms can achieve high accuracy and reliability, their lack of explainability can hinder trust and adoption.

choices.label: C
choices.text: AI technology is susceptible to cyberattacks and hacking.
choices.justification: Although cybersecurity is a concern in the context of AI, it is not directly related to automated decision making.

choices.label: D
choices.text: AI implementation requires significant financial investment.
choices.justification: The financial investment required for AI implementation is a consideration, but is not specific to the concern about automated decision making.

questions.text: Which of the following choices is the MOST effective control that should be implemented to ensure accountability for application users accessing sensitive data in the human resource management system (HRMS) and among interfacing applications to the HRMS?
questions.taskStatement: Utilize technical security testing to identify potential vulnerabilities

choices.label: A
choices.text: Two-factor authentication
choices.justification: Two-factor authentication would enhance security while logging into the human resource management system application; however, it will not establish accountability for actions taken subsequent to login.

choices.label: B
choices.text: A digital certificate
choices.justification: A digital certificate will enhance login security to conclusively authenticate users logging in to the application. However, it will not establish accountability, because user ID and transaction details will not be captured without an audit trail.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Audit trails
choices.justification: Audit trails capture which user performed the transaction, at what time date, and other details, and this helps in establishing accountability among application users.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Single sign-on (SSO) authentication
choices.justification: Single sign-on (SSO) authentication allows users to log in seamlessly to the application, thus easing the authentication process. However, this does not also establish accountability.

questions.text: An information systems (IS) auditor observes large outbound volumes of binary executable data at random intervals. Which of the following is the BEST recommendation for the IS auditor to make?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: Check cloud service provider service level agreements to review storage terms.
choices.justification: Cloud storage service level agreements may not provide the information needed to determine the source of the increase in volume.

choices.label: B
choices.text: historical peak hour timings and system testing schedules.
choices.justification: Although peak hours and system testing generate increased executable data, historical analysis may not suffice to deduce the current peak as an attack or routine.

choices.label: C
choices.text: the firewall logs and network traffic.
choices.justification: Ransomware infections often dump affected data at remote servers before encrypting the victim computers. A large volume of binary executable data can indicate this type of attack. A review of firewall and network logs can give an idea regarding increased traffic.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Check for backup schedules for high volumes of binary executable data.
choices.justification: The backup data may not include the volume information.

questions.text: Which of the following controls would BEST detect intrusion?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's problem and incident management program

choices.label: A
choices.text: User IDs and user privileges are granted through authorized procedures.
choices.justification: User IDs and the granting of user privileges define a policy. This is a type of administrative or managerial control that may prevent intrusion but would not detect it.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Automatic logoff is used when a workstation is inactive for a particular period of time.
choices.justification: Automatic logoff is a method of preventing access through unattended or inactive terminals but is not a detective control.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Automatic logoff of the system occurs after a specified number of unsuccessful attempts.
choices.justification: Unsuccessful attempts to log on are a method for preventing intrusion, not detecting it.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Unsuccessful logon attempts are monitored by the security administrator.
choices.justification: Intrusion is detected by the active monitoring and review of unsuccessful logon attempts.

questions.text: Which of the following activities is MOST important for a security team that uses a security information and event management (SIEM) system to carry out log monitoring and alert generation?
questions.taskStatement: Utilize technical security testing to identify potential vulnerabilities

choices.label: A
choices.text: Periodic analysis of alerts to determine causes of false-positive alerts
choices.justification: Regular, not periodic, analysis of alerts is required to identify and reduce false-positive alerts.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Summarized information about incidents for reporting to management
choices.justification: Summarized information of incidents recorded by the security information and event management (SIEM) is useful in reporting but is a routine activity and not the most important.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Regular analysis of trends found by reviewing generated alerts
choices.justification: Although a SIEM system generates alerts based on log analysis, it may not provide trends, like repetitive failed login attempts for critical system/user errors. These trends indicate possible brute-force attacks or any other attacks that may go undetected in the absence of trend analysis.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Periodic archiving of logs to ensure the expected performance of the system
choices.justification: Archiving logs is part of standard log maintenance.

questions.text: When auditing a role-based access control system, the information systems (IS) auditor noticed that some IT security employees have system administrator privileges on some servers, which allows them to modify or delete transaction logs. Which would be the BEST recommendation that the IS auditor should make?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: Ensure that these employees are adequately supervised.
choices.justification: IT security employees cannot be supervised in the traditional sense unless the supervisor were to monitor each keystroke entered on a workstation, which is not a realistic option.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Ensure that backups of the transaction logs are retained.
choices.justification: Retaining backups of the transaction logs does not prevent the files from unauthorized modification prior to backup.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Implement controls to detect the changes.
choices.justification: The log files themselves are the main evidence that an unauthorized change was made, which is a sufficient detective control. Protecting the log files from modification requires preventive controls, such as securely writing the logs.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Write transaction logs in real time to write once and read many (WORM) drives.
choices.justification: Allowing IT security employees access to transaction logs is often unavoidable because having system administrator privileges is required for them to do their job. The best control in this case, to avoid unauthorized modifications of transaction logs, is to write the transaction logs to write once and read many (WORM) drive media in real time. It is important to note that simply backing up the transaction logs to drives is not adequate because data can be modified prior (typically at night) to the daily backup job execution.

questions.text: A programmer maliciously modified a production program to change data and then restored the program back to the original code. Which of the following would MOST effectively detect the malicious activity?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's log management program

choices.label: A
choices.text: Comparing source code
choices.justification: Source code comparisons are ineffective because the original programs were restored, and the changed program does not exist.

choices.label: B
choices.text: system log files
choices.justification: system log files is the only trail that may provide information about unauthorized activities in the production library.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Comparing object code
choices.justification: Object code comparisons are ineffective because the original programs were restored, and the changed program does not exist.

choices.label: D
choices.text: executable and source code integrity
choices.justification: executable and source code integrity is an ineffective control, because the source code was changed back to the original and will agree with the current executable.

questions.text: Which of the following controls would be MOST effective in reducing the risk of loss due to fraudulent online payment requests?
questions.taskStatement: Utilize technical security testing to identify potential vulnerabilities

choices.label: A
choices.text: Transaction monitoring
choices.justification: An electronic payment system can be the target of fraudulent activities. An unauthorized user can potentially enter false transactions. By monitoring transactions, the payment processor can identify potentially fraudulent transactions based on the typical usage patterns, monetary amounts, physical location of purchases, and other data that are part of the transaction process.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Protecting web sessions using secure sockets layer (SSL)
choices.justification: Using secure sockets layer (SSL) helps to ensure the secure transmission of data to and from the user’s web browser and helps to ensure that the end user has reached the correct web site, but this would not prevent fraudulent transactions.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Enforcing password complexity for authentication
choices.justification: Online transactions are not necessarily protected by passwords; for example, credit card transactions are not necessarily protected. The use of strong authentication helps to protect users of the system from fraud by attackers guessing passwords, but transaction monitoring is the better control.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Inputting validation checks on web forms
choices.justification: Inputting validation checks on web forms is important to ensure that attackers do not compromise the website, but transaction monitoring is the best control.

questions.text: An enterprise determined that its website was compromised, and a rootkit was installed on the server hosting the application. Which of the following choices would have MOST likely prevented the incident?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's information security program

choices.label: A
choices.text: A host-based intrusion prevention system (IPS)
choices.justification: A host-based intrusion prevention system (IPS) prevents unauthorized changes to the host. If a malware attack attempted to install a rootkit, the IPS would refuse to permit the installation without the consent of an administrator.

choices.label: B
choices.text: A network-based intrusion detection system (IDS)
choices.justification: A network-based intrusion detection system (IDS) relies on attack signatures based on known exploits and attack patterns. If the IDS is not kept up-to-date with the latest signatures, or the attacker is able to create or gain access to an exploit unknown to the IDS, it will go undetected. A web server exploit performed through the web application itself, such as a Structured Query Language injection attack, would not appear to be an attack to the network-based IDS.

choices.label: C
choices.text: A firewall
choices.justification: A firewall, by itself, does not protect a web server because the ports required for users to access the web server must be open in the firewall. Web server attacks are typically performed over the same ports that are open for normal web traffic. Therefore, a firewall does not protect the web server.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Operating system (OS) patching
choices.justification: Operating system (OS) patching makes exploitation of the server more difficult for the attacker and less likely. However, attacks on the web application and server OS may succeed based on issues unrelated to any unpatched server vulnerabilities, and the host-based IPS should detect any attempts to change files on the server, regardless of how access was obtained.

questions.text: Which of the following systems or tools can recognize that a credit card transaction is MORE likely to have resulted from a stolen credit card than from the holder of the credit card?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: Intrusion detection systems (IDSs)
choices.justification: An intrusion detection system (IDS) is effective in detecting network or host-based errors but not effective in measuring fraudulent transactions.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Data mining techniques
choices.justification: Data mining is a technique used to detect trends or patterns of transactions or data. If the historical pattern of charges against a credit card account is changed, then it is a flag that the transaction may have resulted from a fraudulent use of the card.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Stateful inspection firewalls
choices.justification: A firewall is an excellent tool for protecting networks and systems but not effective in detecting fraudulent transactions.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Packet filtering routers
choices.justification: A packet filtering router operates at a network level and cannot see a transaction.

questions.text: Which of the following is a MAJOR concern for an information systems (IS) auditor reviewing a security information and event management (SIEM) system that monitors logs from various devices?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's log management program

choices.label: A
choices.text: The number of false-positive alerts generated by correlation rules have decreased.
choices.justification: Reducing the number of false-positive alerts is not a concern but rather a sign that the configuration of the security information and event management (SIEM) is becoming more fine-tuned.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Only security logs from critical systems and devices are collected for monitoring.
choices.justification: Collecting only security logs may be a concern; however, because most critical systems are covered, this is not a major concern.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The security information and event management (SIEM) system has been configured to perform certain tasks automatically based on alerts.
choices.justification: Configuring a SIEM to perform some tasks automatically may not be a concern unless these tasks interrupt business operations.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Correlation rules for monitoring logs are reviewed once upon initial implementation.
choices.justification: Correlation rules are configured based on the nature of risk that the enterprise may want to monitor using a SIEM system. These rules need to be configured, monitored and reviewed periodically to reduce clutter, so that alerts can point out actual errors, events and incidents instead of false-positive alerts. However, a major concern is not reviewing and fine-tuning correlation rules to optimize the SIEM system.

questions.text: An information systems (IS) auditor reviewing a network log discovers that an employee ran elevated commands on their PC by invoking the task scheduler to launch restricted applications. This is an example of what type of attack?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's log management program

choices.label: A
choices.text: Race condition
choices.justification: A race condition exploit involves the timing of two events and an action that causes one event to happen later than expected. The scenario given is not an example of a race condition exploit.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Privilege escalation
choices.justification: A privilege escalation attack obtains higher-level system authority by various methods. In this example, the task scheduler service runs with administrator permissions, and a security flaw allows programs launched by the scheduler to run at the same permission level.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Buffer overflow
choices.justification: Buffer overflows involve applications of actions that take advantage of a defect in the way an application or system uses memory. By overloading the memory storage mechanism, the system will perform in unexpected ways. The scenario given is not an example of a buffer overflow exploit.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Impersonation
choices.justification: Impersonation attacks involve an error in the identification of a privileged user. The scenario given is not an example of this exploit.

questions.text: The MOST serious challenge in the operation of an intrusion detection system is:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's information security program

choices.label: A
choices.text: filtering false-positives alerts.
choices.justification: Because of the configuration and the way intrusion detection system (IDS) technology operates, the main problem in operating IDSs is the recognition (detection) of events that are not really security incidents or false positives. An information systems (IS) auditor needs to be aware of this and should check for implementation of related controls (such as IDS tuning) and incident handling procedures (such as the screening process) to know if an event is a security incident or a false positive.

choices.label: B
choices.text: learning vendor-specific protocols.
choices.justification: It might be necessary to learn vendor-specific protocols or commands for interacting with IDS; however, most vendors provide relevant documentation and trainings, which can be quickly mastered by qualified IT personnel.

choices.label: C
choices.text: updating detection signatures.
choices.justification: It is necessary to regularly update detection signatures; however, the majority of modern IDSs have built-in modules providing automated and secure updates.

choices.label: D
choices.text: blocking eligible connections.
choices.justification: Blocking suspicious connections is a characteristic of intrusion prevention systems, which are different type of network security system.

questions.text: Which of the following is the MOST effective control for restricting access to unauthorized Internet sites in an enterprise?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's log management program

choices.label: A
choices.text: Routing outbound Internet traffic through a content-filtering proxy server
choices.justification: A content-filtering proxy server will effectively monitor user access to Internet sites and block access to unauthorized web sites.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Routing inbound Internet traffic through a reverse proxy server
choices.justification: When a client web browser makes a request to an Internet site, those requests are outbound from the enterprise network. A reverse proxy server is used to allow secure remote connection to an enterprise site, not to control employee web access.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Implementing a firewall with appropriate access rules
choices.justification: A firewall exists to block unauthorized inbound and outbound network traffic. Some firewalls can be used to block or allow access to certain sites, but the term firewall is generic—there are many types of firewalls, and this is not the best answer.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Deploying client software utilities that block inappropriate content
choices.justification: Although client software utilities do exist to block inappropriate content, installing and maintaining additional software on a large number of PCs is less effective than controlling the access from a single, centralized proxy server.

questions.text: An information systems (IS) auditor reviewing a cloud computing environment that is managed by a third party should be MOST concerned when:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's log management program

choices.label: A
choices.text: The enterprise is not permitted to assess the controls in the participating vendor’s site.
choices.justification: The information systems (IS) auditor has no role to play if the contract between the parties does not provide for assessment of controls in the other vendor’s site.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The service level agreement (SLA) does not address the responsibility of the vendor in the case of a security breach.
choices.justification: Administration of cloud computing occurs over the Internet and involves more than one participating entity. It is the responsibility of each of the partners in the cloud computing environment to take care of security issues in their own environments. When there is a security breach, the party responsible for the breach should be identified and made accountable. This is not possible if the service level agreement (SLA) does not address the responsibilities of the partners during a security breach.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Laws and regulations are different in the countries of the enterprise and the vendor.
choices.justification: The IS auditor should ensure that the contract addresses the differing laws and regulations in the countries of the enterprise and the vendor, but having different laws and regulations is not a problem.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The enterprise is using an older version of a browser and is vulnerable to certain types of security risk.
choices.justification: The IS auditor can make suggestions to the audited entity to use appropriate patches or switch over to safer browsers, and then the IS auditor can follow up on the action taken.

questions.text: Which of the following components is responsible for the collection of data in an intrusion detection system?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's threat and vulnerability management program

choices.label: A
choices.text: Analyzer
choices.justification: Analyzers receive input from sensors and determine the presence of and type of intrusive activity.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Administration console
choices.justification: An administration console is the management interface component of an intrusion detection system (IDS).

choices.label: C
choices.text: User interface
choices.justification: A user interface allows the administrators to interact with the IDS.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Sensor
choices.justification: Sensors are responsible for collecting data. Sensors may be attached to a network, server or other location and may gather data from many points for later analysis.

questions.text: During an information systems (IS) audit of a bank, the IS auditor is assessing whether the enterprise properly manages staff member access to the operating system. The IS auditor should determine whether the enterprise performs:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: periodic review of user activity logs.
choices.justification: General operating system access control functions include logging user activities, events, etc. Reviewing these logs may identify users performing activities that should not have been permitted.

choices.label: B
choices.text: verification of user authorization at the field level.
choices.justification: Verification of user authorization at the field level is a database- and/or an application-level access control function and not applicable to an operating system.

choices.label: C
choices.text: review of data communication access activity logs.
choices.justification: of data communication access activity logs is a network control feature.

choices.label: D
choices.text: periodic review of changing data files.
choices.justification: Periodic review of changing data files is related to a change control process.

questions.text: The PRIMARY purpose of audit trails is to:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's information security program

choices.label: A
choices.text: improve response time for users.
choices.justification: The objective of enabling software to provide audit trails is not to improve system efficiency, because it often involves additional processing, which may reduce response time for users.

choices.label: B
choices.text: establish accountability for processed transactions.
choices.justification: Enabling audit trails helps in establishing the accountability and responsibility of processed transactions by tracing transactions through the system.

choices.label: C
choices.text: improve the operational efficiency of the system.
choices.justification: Enabling audit trails involves storage and, thus, occupies disk space and may decrease operational efficiency.

choices.label: D
choices.text: provide information to auditors who want to track transactions.
choices.justification: Audit trails are used to track transactions for various purposes, not just for audit. The use of audit trails for information systems (IS) auditors is valid; however, it is not the primary reason.

questions.text: The network engineer observed surges in logs generated by network activity at arbitrary intervals. Which of the following should the network engineer perform NEXT?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's log management program

choices.label: A
choices.text: Block the excess traffic.
choices.justification: Remote backup generally takes place at a fixed interval, not at an arbitrary time.

choices.label: B
choices.text: and analyze traffic logs.
choices.justification: Further log analysis can suggest whether the network infrastructure is infected by potential malware or a distributed denial of service, which could have caused the surge in logs.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Check with business if transaction volume increased.
choices.justification: Logs need to be analyzed to confirm if an increase in transaction volume caused the surge in logs.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Ask some users to log out.
choices.justification: Peak-hours surges occur generally at fixed time intervals.

questions.text: The GREATEST risk from an improperly implemented intrusion prevention system (IPS) is:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's threat and vulnerability management program

choices.label: A
choices.text: too many alerts for system administrators to verify.
choices.justification: A number of false positives may cause excessive administrator workload, but this is a relatively minor risk.

choices.label: B
choices.text: decreased network performance due to additional traffic.
choices.justification: The intrusion prevention system will not generate any traffic that would impact network performance.

choices.label: C
choices.text: blocking of critical systems or services due to false triggers.
choices.justification: An intrusion prevention system (IPS) prevents a connection or service based on how it is programmed to react to specific incidents. If the IPS is triggered based on incorrectly defined or nonstandard behavior, it may block the service or connection of a critical internal system.

choices.label: D
choices.text: reliance on specialized expertise within the IT organization.
choices.justification: Configuring an IPS can take months of learning what is and what is not acceptable behavior, but this does not require specialized expertise.

questions.text: An information systems (IS) auditor reviewing wireless network security determines that the Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol is disabled at all wireless access points. This practice:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's information security program

choices.label: A
choices.text: reduces the risk of unauthorized access to the network.
choices.justification: Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol (DHCP) automatically assigns internet protocol (IP) addresses to anyone connecting to the network. With DHCP disabled, static IP addresses must be used, and this requires either administrator support or a higher level of technical skill to attach to the network and gain Internet access.

choices.label: B
choices.text: is not suitable for small networks.
choices.justification: DHCP is suitable for networks of all sizes from home networks to large complex organizations.

choices.label: C
choices.text: automatically provides an internet protocol (IP) address to anyone.
choices.justification: DHCP does not provide IP addresses when disabled.

choices.label: D
choices.text: increases the risk associated with Wireless Encryption Protocol.
choices.justification: Disabling the DHCP makes it more difficult to exploit the well-known weaknesses in Wireless Encryption Protocol.

questions.text: Which of the following controls should an information systems (IS) auditor consider when reviewing the integrity of the wireless communications to ensure that the received communications have not been altered in transit?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: Device authentication and data origin authentication
choices.justification: Device authentication and data origin authentication allow wireless endpoints to authenticate each other to prevent man-in-the-middle attacks and masquerading.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Wireless intrusion detection and intrusion prevention systems
choices.justification: Wireless intrusion detection and intrusion prevention systems can detect misconfigured devices and rogue devices and detect, and possibly stop, certain types of attacks.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The use of cryptographic hashes
choices.justification: Calculating cryptographic hashes for wireless communications allows the receiving device to verify that the received communications have not been altered in transit. This prevents masquerading and message modification attacks.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Packet headers and trailers
choices.justification: Packet headers and trailers alone do not ensure that the content has not been altered, because an attacker can alter the data and the trailer.

questions.text: An enterprise is planning to replace its wired networks with wireless networks. Which of the following would BEST secure the wireless network from unauthorized access?
questions.taskStatement: Provide guidance to the organization in order to improve the quality and control of information systems

choices.label: A
choices.text: Implement wired equivalent privacy (WEP).
choices.justification: Wired Equivalent Privacy (WEP) can be cracked within minutes. WEP uses a static key that has to be communicated to all authorized users, thus, management is difficult. Also, there is a greater vulnerability if the static key is not changed at regular intervals.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Permit access to only authorized media access control addresses.
choices.justification: The practice of allowing access based on media access control is not a solution, because media access control (MAC) addresses can be spoofed by attackers to gain access to the network.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Disable open broadcast of service set identifiers.
choices.justification: Disabling open broadcast of service set identifiers is not an effective access control, because many tools can detect a wireless access point that is not broadcasting.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Implement Wi-Fi Protected Access (WPA) 2.
choices.justification: Wi-Fi Protected Access (WPA) 2 implements most of the requirements of the IEEE 802.11i standard. The advanced encryption standard (AES) used in WPA2 provides better security. Also, WPA2 supports the Extensible Authentication Protocol (EAP) and the pre-shared secret key authentication model.

questions.text: Which of the following BEST encrypts data on mobile devices?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: Elliptical curve cryptography
choices.justification: Elliptical curve cryptography (ECC) requires limited bandwidth resources and is suitable for encrypting mobile devices.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Data encryption standard
choices.justification: Data encryption standard uses less processing power when compared with advanced encryption standard (AES), but ECC is more suitable for encrypting data on mobile devices.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Advanced encryption standard
choices.justification: AES is a symmetric algorithm and has the problem of key management and distribution. ECC is an asymmetric algorithm and is better suited for a mobile environment.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The Blowfish algorithm
choices.justification: The use of the Blowfish algorithm consumes too much processing power.

questions.text: A human resources enterprise offers wireless Internet access to its guests, after authenticating with a generic user ID and password. The generic ID and password are requested from the reception desk. Which of the following controls BEST addresses the situation?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: The password for the wireless network is changed on a weekly basis.
choices.justification: Changing the password for the wireless network does not secure against unauthorized access to the enterprise network, especially because a guest can gain access to the wireless local area network at any time prior to the weekly password change interval.

choices.label: B
choices.text: A stateful inspection firewall is used between the public wireless and enterprise networks.
choices.justification: A stateful inspection firewall will screen all packets from the wireless network into the enterprise network; however, the configuration of the firewall needs to be audited and firewall compromises, although unlikely, are possible.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The public wireless network is physically segregated from the enterprise network.
choices.justification: Keeping the wireless network physically separate from the enterprise network is the best way to secure the enterprise network from intrusion.

choices.label: D
choices.text: An intrusion detection system is deployed within the wireless network.
choices.justification: An intrusion detection system will detect intrusions but will not prevent unauthorized individuals from accessing the network.

questions.text: Which of the following is the BEST control over a guest wireless ID that is given to vendor staff?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: Assignment of a renewable user ID that expires daily.
choices.justification: A renewable user ID that expires daily is a good control because it ensures that wireless access will automatically terminate daily and cannot be used without authorization.

choices.label: B
choices.text: A write-once log to monitor the vendor’s activities on the system.
choices.justification: Although it is recommended to monitor vendor activities while vendor staff are on the system, this is a detective control and, thus, is not as strong as a preventive control.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Use of a user ID format similar to that used by employees.
choices.justification: The user ID format does not change the overall security of the wireless connection.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Ensuring that wireless network encryption is configured properly.
choices.justification: Controls related to the encryption of the wireless network are important; however, access to that network is a more critical issue.

questions.text: When auditing an enterprise’s internet of things (IoT) environment, which of the following is a PRIMARY focus area?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate potential opportunities and risks associated with emerging technologies, regulations, and industry practices

choices.label: A
choices.text: Assessing the physical security measures of internet of things (IoT) devices
choices.justification: Physical access to internet of things (IoT) devices can result in the resetting of settings or physical tampering. The compromised IoT device can be used to access other parts of the network. Therefore, assessing the physical security of IoT devices may be relevant when auditing IoT, but evaluating the effectiveness of IoT device authentication controls directly addresses the security aspect of IoT deployments.

choices.label: B
choices.text: the enterprise’s privacy policies related to IoT data
choices.justification: The enterprise’s privacy policies need to take into account the special considerations required to protect the information of individuals from exposure in the IoT environment. Therefore, reviewing the enterprise privacy policies related to IoT data may be relevant when auditing IoT, but evaluating the effectiveness of IoT device authentication controls directly addresses the security aspect of IoT deployments.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Evaluating the effectiveness of IoT device authentication controls
choices.justification: When auditing an enterprise’s IoT environment, the primary focus area is evaluating the effectiveness of IoT device authentication controls. Authentication ensures that only authorized individuals or devices can access and communicate with IoT devices. It is important to assess the strength and implementation of authentication controls to prevent unauthorized access and potential security breaches.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Verifying the availability of IoT device firmware updates
choices.justification: IoT firmware is at the heart of the connected devices and connected products, and, therefore, the availability of firmware updates is important. Verifying the availability of IoT device firmware updates is relevant when auditing IoT, but evaluating the effectiveness of IoT device authentication controls directly addresses the security aspect of IoT deployments.

questions.text: An information systems (IS) auditor is evaluating a virtual-machine-based architecture used for all programming and testing environments. The production architecture is a three-tier physical architecture. What is the MOST important IT control to test to ensure availability and confidentiality of the web application in production?
questions.taskStatement: Provide guidance to the organization in order to improve the quality and control of information systems

choices.label: A
choices.text: Server configuration has been hardened appropriately.
choices.justification: The most important control to test in this configuration is the server configuration hardening. It is important to patch known vulnerabilities and to disable all nonrequired functions before production, especially when production architecture is different from development and testing architecture.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Allocated physical resources are available.
choices.justification: The greatest risk is associated with the difference between the testing and production environments. Ensuring that physical resources are available is a relatively low risk and easily addressed.

choices.label: C
choices.text: System administrators are trained to use the virtual machine (VM) architecture.
choices.justification: Virtual machines (VMs) are often used for optimizing programming and testing infrastructure. In this scenario, the development environment (VM architecture) is different from the production infrastructure (physical three-tier). Because the VMs are not related to the web application in production, there is no real requirement for the system administrators to be familiar with a virtual environment.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The VM server is included in the disaster recovery plan.
choices.justification: Because the VMs are only used in a development environment and not in production, it may not be necessary to include VMs in the disaster recovery plan.

questions.text: Which of the following would be of MOST concern to an information systems (IS) auditor reviewing a virtual private network implementation? Computers on the network are located:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's information security program

choices.label: A
choices.text: on the enterprise’s internal network.
choices.justification: On an enterprise’s internal network, there should be security policies and controls in place to detect and halt an outside attack that uses an internal machine as a staging platform.

choices.label: B
choices.text: at the backup site.
choices.justification: Computers at the backup site are subject to the enterprise security policy and, therefore, are not high-risk computers.

choices.label: C
choices.text: in third-party employees’ homes.
choices.justification: One risk of a virtual private network implementation is the chance of allowing high-risk computers onto the enterprise’s network. All machines that are allowed onto the virtual network should be subject to the same security policy. Home computers of third-party (vendor) employees are least subject to the enterprise security policies and, therefore, are high-risk computers. When a computer is hacked and owned, any network that trusts that computer is at risk. Implementation and adherence to enterprise security policy is easier when all computers on the network are on the enterprise’s campus.

choices.label: D
choices.text: at the enterprise’s remote offices.
choices.justification: Computers on the network that are at the enterprise’s remote offices, perhaps with different information systems (IS) and security employees who have different ideas about security, are higher risk than computers in the main office or backup site but have less risk than home computers.

questions.text: Which of the following is a KEY consideration when implementing machine learning (ML) models for cybersecurity applications?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate potential opportunities and risks associated with emerging technologies, regulations, and industry practices

choices.label: A
choices.text: Regularly updating and retraining the machine learning (ML) model with new data
choices.justification: Regularly updating and retraining the model with new data is crucial when implementing machine learning (ML) models for cybersecurity applications. Cyberthreats and attack patterns evolve, and a static ML model may become less effective in detecting emerging threats. By continuously updating the model with new data, enterprises can improve the ML model accuracy and ensure that it remains effective against evolving threats.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Ensuring the ML model detects all types of threats
choices.justification: Although detecting all types of threats is desirable, it is often not practical or achievable due to the dynamic nature of cyberthreats.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Using open-source ML libraries without considering their security vulnerabilities
choices.justification: Using open-source ML libraries is common and effective, but it is essential to consider and address any potential security vulnerabilities associated with the chosen libraries.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Relying solely on ML models for all cybersecurity decision making
choices.justification: Relying solely on ML models for all cybersecurity decision making is not recommended, because human expertise and contextual analysis are still necessary to validate and interpret model outputs.

category: Information Asset Security Policies, Frameworks, Standards, and Guidelines

questions.text: To ensure compliance with a security policy requiring that passwords be a combination of letters and numbers, an information systems (IS) auditor should recommend that:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: the enterprise policy be changed.
choices.justification: The policy is appropriate and does not require change. Changing the policy would not ensure compliance.

choices.label: B
choices.text: passwords are periodically changed.
choices.justification: Having a requirement to periodically change passwords is good practice and should be in the password policy.

choices.label: C
choices.text: an automated password management tool be used.
choices.justification: The use of an automated password management tool is a preventive control measure. The software prevents repetition (semantic) and enforces syntactic rules, thus making the passwords robust. It also provides a method for ensuring frequent changes and prevents the same user from reusing his/her old password for a designated period of time.

choices.label: D
choices.text: security awareness training is delivered.
choices.justification: Security awareness training does not enforce compliance.

questions.text: The implementation of access controls FIRST requires:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: a classification of information systems (IS) resources.
choices.justification: The first step in implementing access controls is an inventory of information systems (IS) resources, which is the basis for classification.

choices.label: B
choices.text: the labeling of IS resources.
choices.justification: Labeling resources cannot be done without first determining the resources’ classifications.

choices.label: C
choices.text: the creation of an access control list.
choices.justification: The access control list is not done without a meaningful classification of resources.

choices.label: D
choices.text: an inventory of IS resources.
choices.justification: The first step in implementing access controls is an inventory of IS resources, which is the basis for establishing ownership and classification.

questions.text: Enterprise governance of IT frameworks MAINLY helps organizations address business issues by:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the IT strategy for alignment with the organization's strategies and objectives

choices.label: A
choices.text: aligning high-level strategic objectives with operational-level objectives, followed by direct work outcomes.
choices.justification: The strategic alignment between business objectives and enterprise IT objectives is one of the most important advantages of applying enterprise governance of information and technology (EGIT) frameworks because it helps in achieving enterprise objectives and delivering value at a reasonable cost.

choices.label: B
choices.text: establishing a risk management capability to address business issues and preserve the value delivered to stakeholders.
choices.justification: Establishing risk management practices and processes as part of EGIT frameworks is much more effective after aligning the objectives of enterprise IT with the strategic direction of the organization, better preserving the value delivered to stakeholders.

choices.label: C
choices.text: developing a roadmap to help fill in the gap between the current state (as-is) and the desired state (to-be).
choices.justification: Conducting assessments and developing roadmaps comes after establishing strategic alignment between enterprise IT and business strategies.

choices.label: D
choices.text: conducting multiple meetings with different stakeholder groups to learn about their expectations.
choices.justification: Such meetings are a good tool to develop successful enterprise IT strategies but should be done after understanding the business strategies and aligning enterprise IT with them.

questions.text: Users are issued security tokens to be used in combination with a personal identification number (PIN) to access the enterprise virtual private network. Regarding the PIN, what is the MOST important rule to be included in a security policy?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: Users should not leave tokens where they can be stolen.
choices.justification: Access to the token is of no value without the personal identification number (PIN); one cannot work without the other.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Users must never keep the token in the same bag as their laptop computer.
choices.justification: Access to the token is of no value without the PIN; one cannot work without the other.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Users should select a PIN that is completely random, with no repeating digits.
choices.justification: The PIN does not need to be random if it is secret.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Users should never write down their PIN.
choices.justification: If a user writes their PIN on a slip of paper, an individual with the token, the slip of paper and the computer can access the enterprise network. A token and the PIN are a two-factor authentication method.

questions.text: An enterprise has created a policy that defines the types of websites that users are forbidden to access. What is the MOST effective technology to enforce this policy?
questions.taskStatement: Utilize technical security testing to identify potential vulnerabilities

choices.label: A
choices.text: Stateful inspection firewall
choices.justification: A stateful inspection firewall is of little help in filtering web traffic because it does not review the content of the website, nor does it take into consideration the site’s classification.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Web content filter
choices.justification: A web content filter accepts or denies web communications according to the configured rules. To help the administrator properly configure the tool, enterprises and vendors have made available uniform resource locator blacklists and classifications for millions of websites.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Web cache server
choices.justification: A web cache server is designed to improve the speed of retrieving the most common or recently visited web pages.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Proxy server
choices.justification: A proxy server is incorrect because a proxy server services the request of its clients by forwarding requests to other servers. Many people incorrectly use proxy server as a synonym of web proxy server even though not all web proxy servers have content filtering capabilities.

questions.text: Which of the following methods BEST mitigates the risk of disclosing confidential information through the use of social networking sites?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate potential opportunities and risks associated with emerging technologies, regulations, and industry practices

choices.label: A
choices.text: Providing security awareness training
choices.justification: Providing security awareness training is the best method to mitigate the risk of disclosing confidential information on social networking sites. It is important to remember that users may access these services through other means, such as mobile phones and home computers; therefore, awareness training is most critical.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Requiring a signed acceptable use policy
choices.justification: Requiring a signed acceptable use policy can be a good control. However, if users are not aware of the risk, then this policy may not be effective.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Monitoring the use of social media
choices.justification: Monitoring the use of social media through the use of a proxy server that tracks the websites that users visit is not an effective control, because users may access these services through other means, such as mobile phones and home computers.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Blocking access to social media
choices.justification: Blocking the use of social media through network controls is not an effective control, because users may access these services through other means, such as mobile phones and home computers.

questions.text: In an enterprise where an IT security baseline has been defined, an information systems (IS) auditor should FIRST ensure:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: implementation.
choices.justification: The first step is to review the baseline to ensure that it is adequate or sufficient to meet the security requirements of the enterprise. Then, the information systems (IS) auditor ensures that it is implemented and measures compliance.

choices.label: B
choices.text: compliance.
choices.justification: Compliance cannot be measured until the baseline has been implemented, but the IS auditor must first ensure that the correct baseline is being implemented.

choices.label: C
choices.text: documentation.
choices.justification: After the baseline has been defined, it must be documented, and the IS auditor will check that the baseline is appropriate before checking for implementation.

choices.label: D
choices.text: sufficiency.
choices.justification: An IS auditor should first evaluate the definition of the minimum baseline level by ensuring the sufficiency of the control baseline to meet security requirements.

questions.text: Enterprise requirements for complying with adopted global security standards is PRIMARILY determined by:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's information security program

choices.label: A
choices.text: internal compliance.
choices.justification: Complying with adopted global security standards is primarily determined by internal compliance requirements.

choices.label: B
choices.text: legal compliance.
choices.justification: The law does not require adopting global security standards.

choices.label: C
choices.text: regulatory compliance.
choices.justification: Some regulators may require adopting global standards; however, it is not applicable globally.

choices.label: D
choices.text: contractual compliance.
choices.justification: Contracting parties may specify adopting global security standards, but that is not a global requirement.

questions.text: An enterprise can ensure that the recipients of emails from its employees can authenticate the identity of the sender by:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's policies and practices related to asset life cycle management

choices.label: A
choices.text: digitally signing all email messages.
choices.justification: By digitally signing all email messages, the receiver is able to validate the authenticity of the sender.

choices.label: B
choices.text: encrypting all email messages.
choices.justification: Encrypting all email messages ensures that only the intended recipient will be able to open the message; however, it does not ensure the authenticity of the sender.

choices.label: C
choices.text: compressing all email messages.
choices.justification: Compressing all email messages reduces the size of the message but does not ensure authenticity.

choices.label: D
choices.text: password protecting all email messages.
choices.justification: Password protecting all email messages ensures that only those who have the password are able to open the message; however, it does not ensure authenticity of the sender.

questions.text: Which of the following is the MOST effective method for disposing of magnetic media that contains confidential information?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: Degaussing
choices.justification: Degaussing or demagnetizing is a good control, but not sufficient to fully erase highly confidential information from magnetic media.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Defragmenting
choices.justification: The purpose of defragmentation is to improve efficiency by eliminating fragmentation in file systems; it does not remove information.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Erasing
choices.justification: Erasing or deleting magnetic media does not remove the information; this method simply changes a file’s indexing information.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Destroying
choices.justification: Destroying magnetic media is the only way to assure that confidential information cannot be recovered.

questions.text: From a control perspective, the PRIMARY objective of classifying information assets is to:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's information security program

choices.label: A
choices.text: establish guidelines for the level of access controls that should be assigned.
choices.justification: Information has varying degrees of sensitivity and criticality in meeting business objectives. By assigning classes or levels of sensitivity and criticality to information resources, management can establish guidelines for the level of access controls that should be assigned. End user management and the security administrator will use these classifications in their risk assessment process to assign a given class to each asset.

choices.label: B
choices.text: ensure access controls are assigned to all information assets.
choices.justification: Not all information needs to be protected through access controls. Overprotecting data would be expensive.

choices.label: C
choices.text: assist management and auditors in risk assessment.
choices.justification: The classification of information is usually based on the risk assessment, not the other way around.

choices.label: D
choices.text: identify which assets need to be insured against losses.
choices.justification: Insuring assets is valid; however, this is not the primary objective of information classification.

questions.text: Which of the following is the MOST important input for defining an information security framework for an enterprise? Options:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's information security program

choices.label: A
choices.text: Information security policies
choices.justification: Information security policies are part of a framework and defined subsequently.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Regulatory compliance requirements
choices.justification: Defining a security framework may not be specified by regulators.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Risk assessment results
choices.justification: Performing a risk assessment is part of a framework and does not help to define it.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Global security standards
choices.justification: Global security standards provide required high-level essential controls. These assist enterprises to define a security framework that meets the requirements.

questions.text: Which of the following is the BEST audit procedure to determine if a firewall is configured in compliance with the enterprise security policy?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: the parameter settings.
choices.justification: A review of the parameter settings provides a good basis for comparison of the actual configuration to the security policy and provides audit evidence documentation.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Interview the firewall administrator.
choices.justification: An interview with the firewall administrator will not ensure that the firewall is configured correctly.

choices.label: C
choices.text: the actual procedures.
choices.justification: the actual procedures is good but will not ensure that the firewall rules are correct and compliant with policy.

choices.label: D
choices.text: the device’s log file for recent attacks.
choices.justification: Recent attacks may indicate problems with the firewall but will not ensure that it is correctly configured.

questions.text: Which of the following is the MOST effective way to prevent accidental data loss caused by employees?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's data governance program

choices.label: A
choices.text: Blocking access to all external websites and email domains
choices.justification: Blocking access to all external websites and email domains does not prevent all accidental data loss, such as data backup taken following incorrect procedures. The most effective way to prevent such incidents is to educate and train employees on the importance of data protection policies and procedures. Moreover, blocking access to external websites and email domains may hinder business operations and productivity.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Encrypting all sensitive data on all storage devices within the enterprise
choices.justification: Encrypting all sensitive data on all storage devices may not be practical or necessary for all types of data.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Conducting regular user awareness training programs
choices.justification: Accidental data loss caused by employees is a common occurrence in many enterprises. Regular training and awareness programs can help employees understand their roles and responsibilities in protecting sensitive data, identify potential risk and vulnerabilities, and take appropriate measures to prevent data loss incidents. Employee training and awareness are critical to any data protection strategy. Employees need to understand the value of the information that they handle, the consequences of data loss and their role in data protection. Regular training and awareness sessions should be conducted to keep employees updated about best practices for handling and protecting data.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Implementing strict access controls to restrict employee access to sensitive data
choices.justification: Strict access controls can help prevent unauthorized access to sensitive data but may not address the risk of accidental data loss caused by authorized users.

questions.text: Which of the following BEST describes the primary objective of a data loss prevention (DLP) solution?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's data governance program

choices.label: A
choices.text: To prevent any data loss incidents within an enterprise
choices.justification: A data loss prevention (DLP) solution cannot prevent all data loss incidents, because some data loss incidents may still occur despite the implementation of DLP controls.

choices.label: B
