choices.text: To detect and respond to data loss incidents within an enterprise
choices.justification: DLP helps to prevent unintentional or malicious data loss incidents, detect and respond to incidents promptly, and minimize the impact of data loss incidents on the enterprise.

choices.label: C
choices.text: To mitigate the risk of data loss incidents within an enterprise
choices.justification: The primary objective of a DLP solution is to mitigate the risk of data loss incidents by implementing a range of preventive and detective controls, such as access controls, data classification, encryption, monitoring and response procedures. These controls help prevent unintentional or malicious data loss incidents, detect and respond to incidents promptly, and minimize the impact of data loss incidents on the enterprise.

choices.label: D
choices.text: To encrypt data within an enterprise to prevent unauthorized access
choices.justification: DLP does not encrypt enterprise data; rather, it is used to mitigate the risk of data loss incidents by implementing appropriate controls.

questions.text: Which of the following types of risk is BEST represented by an enterprise using manual controls instead of automated controls for data loss prevention?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's data governance program

choices.label: A
choices.text: Control risk
choices.justification: Automated controls are much more effective than manual controls for data loss prevention. Manual controls, therefore, have a higher control risk than automated controls.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Inherent risk
choices.justification: Inherent risk is mitigated by controls, and the absence or weakness of controls may not increase inherent risk.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Detection risk
choices.justification: Detection risk occurs when the auditor fails to detect weakness in control, which is not true in this case.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Sampling risk
choices.justification: Sampling risk is associated with the incorrect assumption of a population and does not apply.

questions.text: Which of the following methods can BEST help prevent inadvertent data loss in an enterprise?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's security awareness training program

choices.label: A
choices.text: Employee training
choices.justification: Employee training is a key component in data loss prevention strategies. Educating employees reduces the likelihood of data loss incidents. Proper training helps employees understand their responsibilities in handling sensitive data and recognizing potential threats; thus, an enterprise can mitigate the risk of unintentional loss caused by human error.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Encryption
choices.justification: Encryption is a crucial method in data loss prevention. It involves converting data into a format that can only be accessed with a decryption key. Encrypted data cannot be accessed by unauthorized individuals without the decryption key even if it is lost or stolen. Although encryption is important for data protection and confidentiality, its primary focus is not the prevention of data loss.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Firewall
choices.justification: Firewalls help protect against external threats by monitoring and blocking unauthorized access. However, they are not directly involved in data loss prevention within an enterprise. The primary purpose of firewalls is to safeguard against unauthorized network access rather than prevent data loss.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Antivirus software
choices.justification: Antivirus software is designed to detect, prevent and remove malware. Although it plays a critical role in protecting systems from various forms of malware, the primary focus of antivirus software is not on preventing data loss directly.

questions.text: Which control is the BEST way to ensure that the data in a file have not been changed during transmission?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: Reasonableness check
choices.justification: A reasonableness check is used to ensure that input data is within expected values, not to ensure integrity of data transmission. Data can be changed and still pass a reasonableness test.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Parity bits
choices.justification: Parity bits are a weak form of data integrity checks used to detect errors in transmission, but they are not as good as using a hash.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Hash values
choices.justification: Hash values are calculated on the file and are very sensitive to any changes in the data values in the file. Thus, they are the best way to ensure that data has not changed.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Check digits
choices.justification: Check digits are used to detect an error in a numeric field such as an account number and is usually related to a transposition or transcribing error.

questions.text: An information systems (IS) auditor is performing a review of a network. Users report that the network is slow and web pages periodically time out. The IS auditor confirms the users’ feedback and reports the findings to the network manager. The most appropriate action for the network management team should be to FIRST:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: use a protocol analyzer to perform network analysis and review error logs of local area network (LAN) equipment.
choices.justification: In this case, the first step is to identify the problem through review and analysis of network traffic. Using a protocol analyzer and reviewing the log files of the related switches or routers determine whether there is a configuration issue or hardware malfunction.

choices.label: B
choices.text: take steps to increase the bandwidth of the connection to the Internet.
choices.justification: Although increasing Internet bandwidth may be required, this may not be needed if the performance issue is due to a different problem or error condition.

choices.label: C
choices.text: create a baseline using a protocol analyzer and implement quality of service to ensure that critical business applications work as intended.
choices.justification: Although creating a baseline and implementing quality of service ensures that critical applications have the appropriate bandwidth, in this case, the performance issue may be related to misconfiguration or equipment malfunction.

choices.label: D
choices.text: implement virtual local area networks (VLANs) to segment the network and ensure performance.
choices.justification: Although implementing virtual local area networks (VLANs) may be a good practice for ensuring adequate performance, in this case, the issue may be related to misconfigurations or equipment malfunction.

questions.text: A cyclic redundancy check is commonly used to determine the:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: accuracy of data input.
choices.justification: Accuracy of data input can be enforced by data validation controls, such as picklists, cross checks, reasonableness checks, control totals and allowed character checks.

choices.label: B
choices.text: integrity of a downloaded program.
choices.justification: A checksum or digital signature is commonly used to validate the integrity of a downloaded program or other transferred data.

choices.label: C
choices.text: adequacy of encryption.
choices.justification: Encryption adequacy is driven by the sensitivity of the data to be protected and algorithms that determine how long it will take to break a specific encryption method.

choices.label: D
choices.text: validity of data transfer.
choices.justification: The accuracy of blocks of data transfers, such as data transfer from hard disks, is validated by a cyclic redundancy check.

questions.text: An enterprise allows for the use of universal serial bus drives to transfer operational data between offices. Which of the following is the GREATEST risk associated with the use of these devices?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: Files not backed up
choices.justification: Although this is a risk, theft of an unencrypted device is a greater risk.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Theft of the devices
choices.justification: Because USB drives tend to be small, they are susceptible to theft or loss. This represents the greatest risk to the enterprise.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Use of the devices for personal purposes
choices.justification: Use of USB drives for personal purposes is a violation of enterprise policy; however, this is not the greatest risk.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Introduction of malware into the network
choices.justification: Good general IT controls include the scanning of USB drives for malware when they are inserted in a computer. The risk of malware in an otherwise robust environment is not as great as the risk of loss or theft.

questions.text: A hard disk containing confidential data was damaged beyond repair. If the goal is to ensure with certainty that access to the data by anyone else is prevented, what should be done to the hard disk before it is discarded?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: Overwriting
choices.justification: Rewriting data is impractical because the hard disk is damaged and offers less assurance than physical destruction even when done successfully.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Low-level formatting
choices.justification: Low-level formatting is impractical because the hard disk is damaged and offers less assurance than physical destruction even when done successfully.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Degaussing
choices.justification: Degaussing is highly effective but offers less assurance than physical destruction.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Destruction
choices.justification: Physically destroying the hard disk is the most effective way to ensure that data cannot be recovered.

questions.text: Which of the following would the auditor consider MOST important for effective data protection using a data loss prevention (DLP) solution?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's data governance program

choices.label: A
choices.text: Ensuring that a single administrator maintains the DLP to prevent data disclosures
choices.justification: Data loss prevention (DLP) helps to protect the enterprise’s sensitive data by applying different controls, and it does not need to be managed by a single administrator.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Ensuring that the DLP solution is properly configured and implemented
choices.justification: A DLP solution is only as effective as its configuration and implementation. If the DLP solution is not properly configured, it may not be able to detect or prevent sensitive data from being disclosed. Additionally, if the DLP solution is not properly implemented, it may be unable to enforce data protection policies effectively.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Ensuring the effectiveness of the enterprise’s DLP policies and procedures
choices.justification: The biggest concern to an IS auditor is to identify whether the data loss prevention (DLP) solution is properly configured and implemented to meet the requirements of the enterprise’s DLP policies and procedures.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Ensuring the effectiveness of the encryption and de-identification technologies
choices.justification: The objective of DLP is not to encrypt or de-identify data; it is used to mitigate data loss incidents by implementing appropriate controls.

questions.text: Which of the following measures is an effective method to deal with a data loss event experienced by an enterprise?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's storage, backup, and restoration policies and processes

choices.label: A
choices.text: Sharing sensitive data via unencrypted email
choices.justification: Sharing sensitive data via unencrypted email is not an effective method for data loss prevention. Email communication is vulnerable to interception and unauthorized access. Without encryption, the data can be easily compromised, leading to data loss or unauthorized disclosure.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Allowing unrestricted access to sensitive data
choices.justification: Allowing unrestricted access to sensitive data is not an effective method for data loss prevention. It increases the risk of data breaches and unauthorized access. By implementing access controls and limiting access to only authorized individuals, enterprises can reduce the potential for data loss or misuse.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Implementing regular data backups
choices.justification: Regular data backups are an effective method for data loss prevention. By creating frequent backups, enterprises can ensure that they can restore the data to its previous state in the event of data loss or corruption. This practice helps protect against accidental data deletion, hardware failure and data breaches.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Using weak passwords for user accounts
choices.justification: Using weak passwords for user accounts is not an effective method for data loss prevention. Weak passwords are easily guessable or susceptible to brute-force attacks, making user accounts vulnerable to unauthorized access. Strong passwords, along with additional security measures, like multifactor authentication, are crucial for protecting sensitive data.

questions.text: Which of the following BEST helps in controlling false-positive alerts received during the implementation of a data loss prevention (DLP) solution?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate data classification practices for alignment with the organization's data governance program, privacy program, and applicable external requirements

choices.label: A
choices.text: Implement the data loss prevention (DLP) solution in monitor-mode.
choices.justification: Implementing monitor-mode allows time to evaluate the alert and make a decision. It will help in controlling interruption in essential communication being blocked due to a false-positive alert but may not control a false positive.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Plan data loss prevention (DLP) implementation in a phased manner.
choices.justification: Implementing a data loss prevention (DLP) solution in a phased manner with a limited number of devices and connections helps in optimizing rules to minimize false-positive alerts.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Appoint an external expert to define rules for the data loss prevention (DLP) solution.
choices.justification: External experts can help in defining rules, but which data need to be protected and how they should be protected can best be defined by internal users and data owners.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Perform data classification before implementing the data loss prevention (DLP) solution.
choices.justification: Performing data classification before considering data loss prevention (DLP) is necessary, but it may not help in controlling false-positive alerts.

questions.text: Which of the following measures is the MOST effective method for preventing data exfiltration?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's information security program

choices.label: A
choices.text: Disabling antivirus software on all devices
choices.justification: Disabling antivirus software on all devices is not an effective method for preventing data exfiltration. Antivirus software plays a crucial role in detecting and mitigating malware and other malicious activities. Disabling it would leave devices vulnerable to various threats, increasing the risk of data exfiltration.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Implementing network segmentation
choices.justification: Implementing network segmentation is an effective method for preventing data exfiltration. Enterprises can isolate sensitive data and limit access to authorized individuals by dividing a network into smaller segments. This helps prevent unauthorized users or malware from moving laterally within the network and accessing valuable data.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Allowing unrestricted USB device usage
choices.justification: Allowing unrestricted USB device usage is not an effective method for preventing data exfiltration. USB devices can be used to copy and transfer sensitive data, intentionally or unintentionally. By enforcing policies that restrict or control USB device usage, enterprises can minimize the risk of unauthorized data transfers.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Using default security configurations
choices.justification: Using default security configurations is not an effective method for preventing data exfiltration. Default configurations often have known vulnerabilities that can be exploited by attackers. It is essential to customize security settings, update default passwords and implement strong security measures to protect against data exfiltration.

questions.text: The PRIMARY purpose of installing data leak prevention software is to:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's information security program

choices.label: A
choices.text: restrict user access to confidential files stored on servers.
choices.justification: Access privileges to confidential files stored on the server is controlled through digital rights management (DRM) software.

choices.label: B
choices.text: detect attempts to destroy sensitive data in an internal network.
choices.justification: Potential attacks to systems on the internal network would normally be controlled through an intrusion detection system (IDS) and an intrusion prevention system (IPS), and by security controls of the systems themselves. Data leak prevention (DLP) systems focus on data leaving the enterprise.

choices.label: C
choices.text: block external systems from accessing internal resources.
choices.justification: Controlling what external systems can access internal resources is the function of a firewall rather than a data loss prevention (DLP) system.

choices.label: D
choices.text: control confidential documents leaving the internal network.
choices.justification: A server running a DLP software application uses predefined criteria to check whether any confidential documents or data are leaving the internal network.

questions.text: Which of the following BEST describes the effect of adopting risk-based audit planning on audit resources? Risk-based auditing helps in:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's data governance program

choices.label: A
choices.text: increasing the frequency of internal audits.
choices.justification: A risk-based audit approach may increase the frequency of audits for high-risk areas but reduces it for low-risk areas.

choices.label: B
choices.text: enabling control self-assessments to replace audits.
choices.justification: Control self-assessments help in reducing the frequency of audits, but they do not replace audits.

choices.label: C
choices.text: reducing the level of detection risk.
choices.justification: A risk-based approach does not reduce detection risk.

choices.label: D
choices.text: allocating audit resources to a higher risk area.
choices.justification: A risk-based audit approach focuses on high-risk areas by allocating audit resources based on priority.

questions.text: Which of the following would be MOST appropriate for helping to detect sensitive information that is stored on the enterprise hard drive(s) with inappropriate authorization or security controls?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's data governance program

choices.label: A
choices.text: intrusion detection system (IDS)
choices.justification: Intrusion detection systems (IDS) identify some sensitive information using signatures built for that purpose, but they are not as effective as data loss prevention (DLP) systems for detecting sensitive information.

choices.label: B
choices.text: data loss prevention (DLP)
choices.justification: Data loss prevention (DLP) systems identify sensitive information stored on endpoint systems or in transit over a network.

choices.label: C
choices.text: intrusion prevention system (IPS)
choices.justification: Intrusion prevention systems (IPSs) prevent sensitive information using signatures built for that purpose. However, they do not identify sensitive information on endpoints or in transit.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Transport Layer Security (TLS)
choices.justification: Transport Layer Security (TLS) is a network encryption protocol used to protect sensitive information, but it does not have any ability to identify sensitive information.

questions.text: An information systems (IS) auditor found that employees are emailing sensitive enterprise information to public web-based email domains. Which of the following is the BEST remediation option for the IS auditor to recommend?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: Encrypted mail accounts
choices.justification: Encrypted email accounts secure the information being sent but do not prevent an employee from sending the information to an unauthorized person.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Training and awareness
choices.justification: Training and awareness may influence employee behavior but are not effective as preventative controls when dealing with intentional exfiltration.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Activity monitoring
choices.justification: Activity monitoring is a detective control and does not prevent data from leaving the network.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Data loss prevention
choices.justification: Data loss prevention is an automated preventive tool that can block sensitive information from leaving the network, while, at the same time, logging the offenders. This is a better choice than relying on training and awareness because it works equally well when there is intent to steal data.

questions.text: Which of the following is the MOST important factor for an enterprise considering implementing a data loss prevention (DLP) solution?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate data classification practices for alignment with the organization's data governance program, privacy program, and applicable external requirements

choices.label: A
choices.text: Evaluating the features of different data loss prevention (DLP) products available
choices.justification: The enterprise should evaluate multiple solutions during the acquisition phase; however, identifying sensitive data is necessary irrespective of product selection.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Understanding the location and nature of sensitive data
choices.justification: It is necessary to identify sensitive data and its location so that an enterprise can evaluate efforts required to protect data. The data should also be classified so that the proper level of protection can be applied to the data.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Educating users on the importance of data protection
choices.justification: Awareness training is part of an overall security program and is not limited to data loss prevention (DLP) solutions.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Determining action to be taken by data loss prevention (DLP) in case of alerts
choices.justification: Action to be taken in case data loss prevention (DLP) finds sensitive data movement may be defined after the sensitive data are identified and located.

questions.text: An alert raised from a data loss prevention (DLP) solution about sensitive data in transit is BEST investigated and resolved by:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate data classification practices for alignment with the organization's data governance program, privacy program, and applicable external requirements

choices.label: A
choices.text: the security officer.
choices.justification: The security officer may not be aware of the nature of data so cannot make a fully informed decision based on the alert.

choices.label: B
choices.text: the data loss prevention (DLP) administrator.
choices.justification: The data loss prevention (DLP) administrator cannot resolve the alert but can act on the data owner’s decision.

choices.label: C
choices.text: the incident response team.
choices.justification: Unless data have leaked, it is not an incident; therefore, the incident response team may not be involved.

choices.label: D
choices.text: the data owner.
choices.justification: The data owner is the best person to make decisions regarding data being sent over networks. Therefore, the data owner should investigate and resolve any alerts from the data loss prevention (DLP) solution.

questions.text: An information systems (IS) auditor is reviewing a third-party agreement for a new cloud-based accounting service provider. Which of the following considerations is the MOST important with regard to the privacy of accounting data?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's privacy program

choices.label: A
choices.text: Data retention, backup and recovery
choices.justification: Data retention, backup and recovery are important controls; however, they do not guarantee data privacy.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Return or destruction of information
choices.justification: When reviewing a third-party agreement, the most important consideration regarding the privacy of the data is the clause concerning the return or secure destruction of information at the end of the contract.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Network and intrusion detection
choices.justification: Network and intrusion detection are helpful when securing the data, but on their own, they do not guarantee data privacy stored at a third-party provider.

choices.label: D
choices.text: A patch management process
choices.justification: A patch management process helps secure servers and may prohibit unauthorized disclosure of data; however, it does not affect the privacy of the data.

questions.text: Which one of the following can be used to provide automated assurance that proper data files are being used during processing?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate potential opportunities and risks associated with emerging technologies, regulations, and industry practices

choices.label: A
choices.text: File header record
choices.justification: A file header record provides assurance that proper data files are being used, and it allows for automatic checking.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Version usage
choices.justification: Although version usage provides assurance that the correct file and version are being used, it does not allow for automatic checking.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Parity checking
choices.justification: Parity checking is a data integrity validation method typically used by a data transfer program. Although parity checking may help to ensure that data and program files are transferred successfully, it does not help to ensure that the proper data or program files are being used.

choices.label: D
choices.text: File security controls
choices.justification: File security controls cannot be used to provide assurance that proper data files are being used and cannot allow for automatic checking.

questions.text: An information systems (IS) auditor discovers that the chief information officer (CIO) of an enterprise is using a wireless broadband modem using global system for mobile communications (GSM) technology. This modem is being used to connect the CIO’s laptop to the enterprise virtual private network when the CIO travels outside of the office. The IS auditor should:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: do nothing because the inherent security features of GSM technology are appropriate.
choices.justification: The inherent security features of global system for mobile communications (GSM) technology combined with the use of a virtual private network (VPN) are appropriate. The confidentiality of the communication on the GSM radio link is ensured by the use of encryption, and the use of a VPN signifies that an encrypted session is established between the laptop and the enterprise network. GSM is a global standard for cellular telecommunications that can be used for voice and data. Currently deployed commercial GSM technology has multiple overlapping security features that prevent eavesdropping, session hijacking and unauthorized use of the GSM carrier network. While other wireless technologies such as 802.11 wireless local area network (LAN) technologies have been designed to allow the user to adjust or even disable security settings, GSM does not allow any devices to connect to the system unless all relevant security features are active and enabled.

choices.label: B
choices.text: recommend that the CIO stop using the laptop computer until encryption is enabled.
choices.justification: Because the chief information officer (CIO) is using a VPN, it can be assumed that encryption is enabled in addition to the security features in GSM. VPNs do not allow the transfer of data for storage on the remote device (such as the CIO’s laptop).

choices.label: C
choices.text: ensure that media access control (MAC) address filtering is enabled on the network so that unauthorized wireless users cannot connect.
choices.justification: Media access control (MAC) filtering can be used on a wireless LAN but does not apply to a GSM network device.

choices.label: D
choices.text: suggest that multifactor authentication be used over the wireless link to prevent unauthorized communications.
choices.justification: Because the GSM network is being used rather than a wireless LAN, it is not possible to configure settings for multifactor authentication over the wireless link. However, in other technology configurations, multifactor authentication is recommended because it will better protect against unauthorized access than single-factor authentication.

questions.text: An information systems (IS) auditor performing an audit has determined that developers have been granted administrative access to the virtual machine management console to manage their own servers used for software development and testing. Which of the following choices is the PRIMARY concern for the IS auditor?
questions.taskStatement: Provide guidance to the organization in order to improve the quality and control of information systems

choices.label: A
choices.text: Developers can create or de-provision servers.
choices.justification: Virtualization offers the ability to create or destroy virtual machine (VM) through the administrative interface with administrative access. Although a developer is unlikely to de-provision a production server, the administrative console would grant them the ability to do this, which would be a significant risk.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Developers can gain elevated access to production servers.
choices.justification: When properly configured, the administrative console of a virtual server host does not allow an individual to bypass the authentication of the guest operating system (OS) to access the server. In this case, although the developers can potentially start, stop or even de-provision a production VM, they cannot gain elevated access to the OS of the guest through the administrative interface.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Developers can affect the performance of production servers with their applications.
choices.justification: Although there can be instances where a software development team might use resource-intensive applications that can cause performance issues for the virtual host, the greater risk is the ability to de-provision VMs.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Developers can install unapproved applications to any servers.
choices.justification: When properly configured, the administrative console of a virtual server host does not allow an individual to bypass the authentication of the guest OS to access the server; therefore, the concern that unauthorized software can be installed is not valid.

questions.text: An enterprise is reviewing its contract with a cloud computing provider. For which of the following reasons would the enterprise want to remove a lock-in clause from the cloud service contract?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate whether IT vendor selection and contract management processes meet business, legal, and regulatory requirements

choices.label: A
choices.text: Availability
choices.justification: Removing the customer lock-in clause will not secure availability of the systems resources stored in a cloud computing environment.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Portability
choices.justification: When drawing up a contract with a cloud service provider, the ideal practice is to remove the customer lock-in clause. It may be important for the client to secure portability of their system assets (i.e., the right to transfer from one vendor to another).

choices.label: C
choices.text: Agility
choices.justification: Agility refers to efficiency of solutions enabling enterprises to respond to business needs faster. This is a desirable quality of cloud computing.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Scalability
choices.justification: Scalability is the strength of cloud computing through the ability to adjust service levels according to changing business circumstances. Therefore, this is not the best option.

questions.text: An enterprise’s IT director approved the installation of a wireless local area network (WLAN) access point in a conference room for a team of consultants to access the Internet with their laptop computers. The BEST control to protect the corporate servers from unauthorized access is to ensure that:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: encryption is enabled on the access point.
choices.justification: Enabling encryption is a good idea to prevent unauthorized network access, but it is more important to isolate the consultants from the rest of the corporate network.

choices.label: B
choices.text: the conference room network is on a separate virtual local area network (VLAN).
choices.justification: The installation of the wireless network device presents risk to the corporate servers from authorized and unauthorized users. A separate virtual local area network (VLAN) is the best solution because it ensures that authorized and unauthorized users are prevented from gaining network access to database servers, while allowing Internet access to authorized users.

choices.label: C
choices.text: antivirus signatures and patch levels are current on the consultants’ laptops.
choices.justification: Antivirus signatures and patch levels are good practices but not as critical as preventing network access via access controls for the enterprise’s servers.

choices.label: D
choices.text: default user IDs are disabled and strong passwords are set on the corporate servers.
choices.justification: Protecting the enterprise’s servers through good passwords is good practice, but it is still necessary to isolate the network being used by the consultants. If the consultants can access the rest of the network, they can use password cracking tools against other corporate machines.

questions.text: An enterprise is planning to deploy an outsourced cloud-based application that is used to track job applicant data for the human resources department. Which of the following should be the GREATEST concern to an information systems (IS) auditor?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate whether IT vendor selection and contract management processes meet business, legal, and regulatory requirements

choices.label: A
choices.text: The service level agreement (SLA) ensures strict limits for uptime and performance.
choices.justification: Although this application may have strict requirements for availability, it is assumed that the service level agreement (SLA) would contain these same elements; therefore, this is not a concern.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The cloud provider will not agree to an unlimited right-to-audit as part of the SLA.
choices.justification: The right-to-audit clause is good to have, but there are limits on how a cloud service provider may interpret this requirement. The task of reviewing and assessing all the controls in place at a multinational cloud provider would likely be a costly and time-consuming exercise; therefore, such a requirement may be of limited value.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The SLA is not explicit regarding the disaster recovery plan capabilities of the cloud provider.
choices.justification: Because the SLA normally specifies uptime requirements, the means used to achieve those goals (which include the specific disaster recovery plan capabilities of the provider) are typically not reviewed in-depth by the customer, nor are they typically specified in an SLA.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The cloud provider’s physical data centers are in multiple cities and countries.
choices.justification: Having data in multiple countries is the greatest concern because HR applicant data can contain personally identifiable information. There may be legal compliance issues if these data are stored in a country with different laws regarding data privacy. Although the enterprise is bound by the privacy laws where it is based, it may not have legal recourse if a data breach happens in a jurisdiction where the same laws do not apply.

questions.text: Which of the following should be a concern for an information systems (IS) auditor reviewing an enterprise’s cloud computing strategy that is based on a software as a service (SaaS) model with an external provider?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: Workstation upgrades must be performed.
choices.justification: Unless organization workstations are obsolete, upgrading should not be an issue with a software as a service (SaaS) model, because most applications running as SaaS use common technologies that allow a user to run the software on different devices.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Long-term software acquisition costs are higher.
choices.justification: The reduction of software acquisition costs is one of the benefits of SaaS.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Contract with the provider does not include onsite technical support.
choices.justification: A SaaS provider does not normally have onsite support for the enterprise.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Incident handling procedures with the provider are not well defined.
choices.justification: A SaaS provider does not normally have onsite support for the enterprise. Therefore, incident handling procedures between the enterprise and its provider are critical for the detection, communication and resolution of incidents, including effective lines of communication and escalation processes.

questions.text: Which of the following is the responsibility of an infrastructure-as-a-service cloud provider during an audit of third-party management programs?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate potential opportunities and risks associated with emerging technologies, regulations, and industry practices

choices.label: A
choices.text: Security settings for the operating system
choices.justification: Security settings for the operating system are the responsibility of the customer.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Maintenance of the host firewall
choices.justification: Firewall settings are the responsibility of the customer.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Security settings and maintenance of the hypervisor
choices.justification: For infrastructure as a service, the cloud provider is responsible for maintenance and security settings for the hypervisor and below. The cloud provider manages the infrastructure, while the customer purchases, installs, configures and manages software, which includes operating systems, middleware and applications installed above the hypervisor level.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Access control configuration for servers
choices.justification: Access control configuration for servers is the responsibility of the customer.

questions.text: Which of the following is the MOST important security consideration to an enterprise that wants to move a business application to external cloud service (PaaS) provided by a vendor?
questions.taskStatement: Provide guidance to the organization in order to improve the quality and control of information systems

choices.label: A
choices.text: Classification and categories of data processed by the application
choices.justification: Types of data and their sensitivity is a primary consideration because there might be legal obligations related to data hosting and its level of protection (e.g., personal information, banking information and health information).

choices.label: B
choices.text: Cost of hosting the application internally versus externally
choices.justification: Cost is an important factor for an enterprise to consider during the move to cloud, however the highest risk is to violate data privacy laws.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Reputation of a vendor based on the market and feedback from clients
choices.justification: The reputation of a vendor on the market is an important factor for an enterprise to consider during the move to cloud, however the highest risk is to violate data privacy laws.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Drop in application performance due to use of shared services
choices.justification: The drop in application performance due to use of shared services is an important factor for an enterprise to consider during the move to cloud, however the highest risk is to violate data privacy laws.

questions.text: When planning an audit of a network setup, an information systems (IS) auditor should give highest priority to obtaining which of the following network documentation?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's information security program

choices.label: A
choices.text: Wiring and schematic diagram
choices.justification: The wiring and schematic diagram of the network is necessary to carry out a network audit. The information systems (IS) auditor needs to know what equipment, configuration and addressing is used on the network to perform an audit of the network setup.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Users’ lists and responsibilities
choices.justification: When performing an audit of network setup, the users’ lists would not be of value.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Application lists and their details
choices.justification: Application lists are not required to audit network configuration.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Backup and recovery procedures
choices.justification: Backup and recovery procedures are important but not as important as knowing the network layout.

questions.text: An enterprise is implementing a Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol (DHCP). Which of the following conditions represents the GREATEST concern?
questions.taskStatement: Utilize technical security testing to identify potential vulnerabilities

choices.label: A
choices.text: Most employees use laptops.
choices.justification: Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol (DHCP) provides convenience (an advantage) to laptop users.

choices.label: B
choices.text: A packet filtering firewall is used.
choices.justification: The existence of a firewall can be a security measure and is not normally be of concern.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The internet protocol (IP) address space is smaller than the number of PCs.
choices.justification: A limited number of internet protocol (IP) addresses can be addressed through network address translation or by increasing the number of IP addresses assigned to a particular subnet.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Access to a network port is not restricted.
choices.justification: Physical access to network ports is not restricted, allowing unauthorized individuals to connect to the internal network.

questions.text: To prevent internet protocol (IP) spoofing attacks, a firewall should be configured to drop a packet for which the sender of a packet:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: specifies the route that a packet should take through the network (the source routing field is enabled).
choices.justification: internet protocol (IP) spoofing takes advantage of the source-routing option in the IP. With this option enabled, an attacker can insert a spoofed source IP address. The packet will travel the network according to the information within the source-routing field, bypassing the logic in each router, including dynamic and static routing.

choices.label: B
choices.text: puts multiple destination hosts (the destination field has a broadcast address).
choices.justification: If a packet has a broadcast destination address, it is suspicious and, if allowed to pass, will be sent to all addresses in the subnet. This is not related to IP spoofing.

choices.label: C
choices.text: indicates that the computer should immediately stop using the transmission control protocol (TCP) connection (a reset flag is turned on).
choices.justification: Turning on the reset flag is part of the normal procedure to end a transmission control protocol (TCP) connection.

choices.label: D
choices.text: allows use of dynamic routing instead of static routing (Open Shortest Path First protocol is enabled).
choices.justification: The use of dynamic or static routing does not represent a spoofing attack.

questions.text: An information systems (IS) auditor performing an audit of the newly installed Voice-over Internet Protocol system is inspecting the wiring closets on each floor of a building. What would be the GREATEST concern?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: The local area network (LAN) switches are not connected to uninterruptible power supply units.
choices.justification: Voice-over Internet Protocol (VoIP) telephone systems use standard network cabling and typically each telephone gets power over the network cable (power over Ethernet) from the wiring closet where the network switch is installed. If the local area network switches do not have backup power, the phones will lose power if there is a utility interruption and potentially not be able to make emergency calls.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Network cabling is disorganized and not properly labeled.
choices.justification: Although improper cabling can create reliability issues, the more critical issue in this case is the lack of power protection.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The telephones are using the same cable used for LAN connections.
choices.justification: An advantage of VoIP telephone systems is that they use the same cable types and network switches as standard PC network connections. Therefore, this is not a concern.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The wiring closet also contains power lines and breaker panels.
choices.justification: If the power and telephone equipment are separated, this is not a significant risk.

questions.text: Which of the following is the BEST way to minimize unauthorized access to unattended end-user PC systems?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's threat and vulnerability management program

choices.label: A
choices.text: Enforce the use of a password-protected screen saver.
choices.justification: A password-protected screen saver with a proper time interval is the best measure to prevent unauthorized access to unattended end-user systems. It is important to ensure that users lock the workstation when they step away from the machine, which is something that can be reinforced via awareness training.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Implement a proximity-based authentication system.
choices.justification: There are solutions that will lock machines when users step away from their desks, and those are suitable here; however, those tools are a more expensive solution that normally include the use of smart cards and extra hardware. Therefore, the use of a password-protected screen saver is a better solution.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Terminate the user session at predefined intervals.
choices.justification: Terminating user sessions is often done for remote login (periodic re-authentication) or after a certain amount of inactivity on a web or server session. There is more risk related to leaving the workstation unlocked.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Adjust power management settings so the monitor screen is blank.
choices.justification: Switching off the monitor is not a solution, because the monitor can simply be switched on.

questions.text: The information systems (IS) management of a multinational enterprise is considering upgrading its existing virtual private network (VPN) to support Voice-over Internet Protocol (VoIP) communication via tunneling. Which of the following considerations should be PRIMARILY addressed?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: Reliability and quality of service
choices.justification: Reliability and quality of service (QoS) are the primary considerations to be addressed. Voice communications require consistent levels of service, which may be provided through QoS and class of service controls.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Means of authentication
choices.justification: The enterprise currently has a virtual private network (VPN); authentication has been implemented by the VPN using tunneling.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Privacy of voice transmissions
choices.justification: Privacy of voice transmissions is provided by the VPN protocol.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Confidentiality of data transmissions
choices.justification: The enterprise currently has a VPN; confidentiality of data and Voice-over Internet Protocol traffic has been implemented by the VPN using tunneling.

questions.text: Which of the following is the GREATEST concern associated with the use of peer-to-peer computing?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: Virus infection
choices.justification: Although peer-to-peer computing does increase the risk of virus infection, the risk of data leakage is more severe, especially if it contains proprietary data or intellectual property.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Data leakage
choices.justification: Peer-to-peer computing can share the contents of a user hard drive over the Internet. The risk that sensitive data can be shared with others is the greatest concern.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Network performance issues
choices.justification: Peer-to-peer computing may use more network bandwidth and, therefore, may create performance issues. However, data leakage is a more severe risk.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Unauthorized software usage
choices.justification: Peer-to-peer computing may be used to download or share unauthorized software, which users can install on their PCs unless other controls prevent it. However, data leakage is a more severe risk.

questions.text: Which of the following is the MOST secure and economical method for connecting a private network over the Internet in a small- to medium-sized enterprise?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: Virtual private network (VPN)
choices.justification: The most secure method is a virtual private network (VPN), using encryption, authentication and tunneling to allow data to travel securely from a private network to the Internet.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Dedicated line
choices.justification: A dedicated line is quite expensive and only needed when there are specific confidentiality and availability needs.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Leased line
choices.justification: A leased line is an expensive but private option, but rarely a good option today.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Integrated services digital network
choices.justification: Integrated services digital network is not encrypted and needs additional security to be a valid option.

questions.text: What is the MOST prevalent security risk when an organization implements remote virtual private network (VPN) access to its network?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: Malicious code can be spread across the network.
choices.justification: Virtual private network (VPN) is a mature technology; VPN devices are hard to break. However, when remote access is enabled, malicious code in a remote client can spread to the enterprise’s network. One problem is when the VPN terminates inside the network and the encrypted VPN traffic goes through the firewall. This means that the firewall cannot adequately examine the traffic.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The VPN logon can be spoofed.
choices.justification: A secure VPN solution uses two-factor authentication to prevent spoofing.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Traffic can be sniffed and decrypted.
choices.justification: Sniffing encrypted traffic does not generally provide an attack vector for its unauthorized decryption.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The VPN gateway can be compromised.
choices.justification: A misconfigured or poorly implemented VPN gateway can be subject to attack, but if it is located in a secure subnet, then the risk is reduced.

questions.text: An enterprise provides information to its supply chain partners and customers through an extranet infrastructure. Which of the following should be the GREATEST concern to an information systems (IS) auditor reviewing the firewall security architecture?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: A secure sockets layer (SSL) has been implemented for user authentication and remote administration of the firewall.
choices.justification: Using secure sockets layer (SSL) for firewall administration is important because changes in user and supply chain partners’ roles and profiles will be dynamic.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Firewall policies are updated based on changing requirements.
choices.justification: It is appropriate to maintain the firewall policies as needed.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Inbound traffic is blocked unless the traffic type and connections have been specifically permitted.
choices.justification: It is prudent to block all inbound traffic to an extranet unless permitted.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The firewall is placed on top of the commercial operating system (OS) with all default installation options.
choices.justification: The greatest concern when implementing firewalls on top of commercial operating systems (OSs) is the potential presence of vulnerabilities that can undermine the security posture of the firewall platform itself. In most circumstances, when commercial firewalls are breached, that breach is facilitated by vulnerabilities in the underlying OS. Keeping all installation options available on the system further increases the risk of vulnerabilities and exploits.

questions.text: An IT auditor completed the fieldwork phase of a network security review and is preparing the initial draft of the audit report. Which of the following findings would be the BIGGEST risk to the enterprise?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate whether IT operations and maintenance practices support the organization's objectives

choices.label: A
choices.text: Network penetration tests are performed by an internal team.
choices.justification: Network penetration testing can be performed by an internal team if the remediation activities are tracked properly.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Network firewall rules are not approved by the chief information security officer (CISO) before implementation.
choices.justification: Network firewall rules are not always required to be approved by the chief information security officer (CISO); they can be approved by someone who understands the business requirements for rule implementation.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Network penetration tests are not performed.
choices.justification: Not performing penetration tests is a risk to network security, but lack of a complete asset inventory and their criticality is a bigger risk.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The inventory of network devices was last updated two years ago.
choices.justification: Keeping an up-to-date asset inventory is the most important requirement to keep an enterprise’s information assets secure. Without a complete inventory list and asset criticality determination, the risk assessment cannot be completed and controls will be inadequate.

questions.text: Which of the following would be the BEST overall control for an Internet business looking for confidentiality, reliability and integrity of data?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: Transport Layer Security (TLS)
choices.justification: Transport Layer Security (TLS) is used for many ecommerce applications to set up a secure channel for communications that provides confidentiality through a combination of public and symmetric key encryption and integrity through hash message authentication code.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Intrusion detection system (IDS)
choices.justification: An intrusion detection system (intrusion detection system (IDS)) will log network activity but is not used for protecting traffic over the Internet.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Public key infrastructure
choices.justification: Public key infrastructure is used in conjunction with secure sockets layer (SSL) or for securing communications such as ecommerce and email.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Virtual private network (VPN)
choices.justification: A virtual private network (VPN) is a generic term for a communications tunnel that can provide confidentiality, integrity and authentication (reliability). A VPN can operate at different levels of the Open Systems Interconnection stack and may not always be used in conjunction with encryption. SSL can be called a type of VPN.

questions.text: Which of the following provides the MOST relevant information for proactively strengthening security settings?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: Bastion host
choices.justification: A bastion host is a hardened system used to host services. It does not provide information about an attack.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Intrusion detection system (IDS)
choices.justification: Intrusion detection systems (IDSs) are designed to detect and address an attack in progress and stop it as soon as possible.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Honeypot
choices.justification: The design of a honeypot is such that it lures the hacker and provides clues about the hacker’s methods and strategies, and the resources required to address such attacks. A honeypot allows the attack to continue, to obtain information about the hacker’s strategy and methods.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Intrusion prevention system (IPS)
choices.justification: Intrusion prevention systems (IPS) are designed to detect and address an attack in progress and stop it as soon as possible.

questions.text: The technique used to ensure security in virtual private networks is called:
questions.taskStatement: Utilize technical security testing to identify potential vulnerabilities

choices.label: A
choices.text: data encapsulation.
choices.justification: Encapsulation, or tunneling, is a technique used to encrypt the traffic payload so that it can be securely transmitted over an insecure network.

choices.label: B
choices.text: data wrapping.
choices.justification: Wrapping is used where the original packet is wrapped in another packet but is not directly related to security.

choices.label: C
choices.text: data transformation.
choices.justification: To transform or change the state of communication is not used for security.

choices.label: D
choices.text: data hashing.
choices.justification: Hashing is used in virtual private networks to ensure message integrity.

questions.text: An information systems (IS) auditor is reviewing a manufacturing enterprise and finds that mainframe users at a remote site connect to the mainframe at headquarters over the Internet via Telnet. Which of the following offers the STRONGEST security?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: Use of a point-to-point leased line
choices.justification: A leased line effectively extends the local area network of the headquarters to the remote site, and the mainframe Telnet connection travels over the private line, which is less of a security risk when using an insecure protocol such as Telnet.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Use of a firewall rule to allow only the internet protocol (IP) address of the remote site
choices.justification: A firewall rule at the headquarters network to only allow Telnet connections from the internet protocol (IP) address assigned to the remote site makes the connection more secure than the current arrangement, but a dedicated leased line is the most secure option of those listed.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Use of two-factor authentication
choices.justification: Although two-factor authentication enhances the login security, it does not secure the transmission channel against eavesdropping, and, therefore, a leased line is a better option.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Use of a nonstandard port for Telnet
choices.justification: Attacks on network services start with the assumption that network services use the standard Transmission Control Protocol/Internet Protocol (TCP/IP) port number assigned for the service, which is port 23 for Telnet. By reconfiguring the host and client, a different port can be used. Assigning a nonstandard port for services is a good general security practice because it makes it more difficult to determine what service is using the port; however, in this case, creating a leased-line connection to the remote site is a better solution.

questions.text: Which of the following MOST effectively enhances the security of a challenge-response based authentication system?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: Selecting a more robust algorithm to generate challenge strings
choices.justification: Selecting a more robust algorithm enhances the security; however, this may not be as important in terms of risk mitigation when compared to man-in-the-middle attacks.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Implementing measures to prevent session hijacking attacks
choices.justification: Challenge-response-based authentication is prone to session hijacking or man-in-the-middle attacks. Security management should be aware of this and engage in risk assessment and control design such as periodic authentication when they employ this technology.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Increasing the frequency of associated password changes
choices.justification: Frequently changing passwords is a good security practice; however, the exposures lurking in communication pathways may pose a greater risk.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Increasing the length of authentication strings
choices.justification: Increasing the length of authentication strings will not prevent man-in-the-middle or session hijacking attacks.

questions.text: Which of the following types of transmission media provide the BEST security against unauthorized access?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's change, configuration, release, and patch management programs

choices.label: A
choices.text: Copper wire
choices.justification: Twisted pair, coaxial and copper wire traffic can be monitored with inexpensive equipment.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Shielded twisted pair
choices.justification: Twisted pair cabling is a form of copper wire, and although shielding affords some degree of protection from interference, it does not improve security against unauthorized access.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Fiber-optic cables
choices.justification: Fiber-optic cables have proven to be more secure and more difficult to tap than the other media.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Coaxial cables
choices.justification: Coaxial cable can be monitored with relative ease.

questions.text: In a small enterprise, an employee performs computer operations and, when the situation demands, the program modifications. Considering the lack of separation of duties in the IT environment, which of the following should the information systems (IS) auditor recommend to the IT management to mitigate the risk?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's information security program

choices.label: A
choices.text: Automated logging of changes to development libraries
choices.justification: Logging of changes to production libraries is a good practice, but, because the administrator can alter the logs, this is not a sufficient control.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Additional staff to provide segregation/separation of duties (SoD)
choices.justification: Although adherence to segregation/separation of duties (SoD) and recruitment of additional staff are preferred , this practice is not always possible in small enterprises.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Procedures that verify that only approved program changes are implemented
choices.justification: An information systems (IS) auditor must consider recommending a better process. An IS auditor should recommend a formal change control process that manages and can detect changes to production source and object code, such as code comparisons, so the changes can be reviewed on a regular basis by a third party. This is a compensating control process.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Access controls to prevent the operator from making program modifications
choices.justification: Requiring a third party to do the changes may not be practical in a small enterprise where another person with adequate expertise may not be available.

questions.text: Which of the following types of firewalls would BEST protect a network from an Internet attack?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: Screened subnet firewall
choices.justification: A screened subnet firewall would provide the best protection. The screening router can be a commercial router or a node with routing capabilities and the ability to allow or avoid traffic between nets or nodes based on addresses, ports, protocols, interfaces, etc. The subnet would isolate Internet-based traffic from the rest of the enterprise network.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Application filtering gateway
choices.justification: Application-level gateways are mediators between two entities that want to communicate, also known as proxy gateways. The application level (proxy) works at the application level, not just at a packet level. This is the best solution to protect an application but not a network.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Packet filtering router
choices.justification: A packet filtering router examines the header of every packet or data traveling between the Internet and the enterprise network. This is a low-level control.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Circuit-level gateway
choices.justification: A circuit level gateway, such as a Socket Secure server, will protect users by acting as a proxy but is not the best defense for a network.

questions.text: The IT team of an enterprise informs the information systems (IS) auditor of a concern that some users might be loading illegal software packages onto a network. Which of the following should the IS auditor recommend for identifying if the concern is valid?
questions.taskStatement: Provide guidance to the organization in order to improve the quality and control of information systems

choices.label: A
choices.text: Use of diskless workstations
choices.justification: Diskless workstations act as a preventive control and are not totally effective in preventing users from accessing illegal software over the network.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Periodic checking of hard drives
choices.justification: The periodic checking of hard drives would be the most effective method of identifying illegal software packages loaded onto the network.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Use of current antivirus software
choices.justification: Antivirus software will not necessarily identify illegal software unless the software contains a virus.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Policies that result in instant dismissal if violated
choices.justification: Policies are a preventive control to lay out the rules about loading the software but will not detect the actual occurrence.

questions.text: Enterprise XYZ has outsourced production support to service provider ABC, located in another country. The ABC service provider personnel remotely connect to the enterprise network of the XYZ outsourcing entity over the Internet. Which of the following would BEST provide assurance that transmission of information is secure while the production support team at ABC is providing support to XYZ?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: Secret key encryption
choices.justification: Secret key encryption requires sharing of the same key at the source and destination and involves an additional step for encrypting and decrypting data at each end. This is not a feasible solution given the scenario.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Dynamic internet protocol (IP) address and port
choices.justification: Using a dynamic internet protocol (IP) address and port is not an effective control, because an attacker can easily find the new address using the domain name system.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Hash functions
choices.justification: Although the use of a cryptographic hash function may be helpful to validate the integrity of data files, in this case, it is not useful for a production support team connecting remotely.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Virtual private network (VPN) tunnel
choices.justification: Because ABC and XYZ are communicating over the Internet, which is an untrusted network, establishing an encrypted virtual private network (VPN) tunnel best ensures that the transmission of information is secure.

questions.text: The PRIMARY goal of a website certificate is:
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: authentication of the website that will be surfed.
choices.justification: Authenticating the site to be surfed is the primary goal of a web certificate.

choices.label: B
choices.text: authentication of the user who surfs through that site.
choices.justification: Authentication of a user is achieved through passwords and not by a website certificate.

choices.label: C
choices.text: preventing surfing of the website by hackers.
choices.justification: The site certificate does not prevent hacking, nor does it authenticate a person.

choices.label: D
choices.text: the same purpose as that of a digital certificate.
choices.justification: Website certificates may serve the same purpose as a digital certificate, but the goal of certificates is authentication.

questions.text: An organization is considering connecting a critical PC-based system to the Internet. Which of the following provides the BEST protection against hacking?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: Application-level gateway
choices.justification: An application-level gateway is the best way to protect against hacking because it can be configured with detailed rules that describe the type of user or connection that is or is not permitted. It analyzes, in detail, each package—not only in layers one through four of the Open System Interconnection model, but also layers five through seven, which means that it reviews the commands of each higher-level protocol (Hypertext Transmission Protocol, File Transfer Protocol, Simple Network Management Protocol, etc.).

choices.label: B
choices.text: Remote access server
choices.justification: For a remote access server, there is a device (server) that asks for a username and password before entering the network. This is good when accessing private networks, but it can be mapped or scanned from the Internet, creating security exposure.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Proxy server
choices.justification: Proxy servers can provide excellent protection, but depending on the type of proxy, they may not be able to examine traffic as effectively as an application gateway. Proxy servers require experienced personnel to manage them. Applications can use different ports for the different sections of the program.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Port scanning
choices.justification: Port scanning is used to detect vulnerabilities or open ports on a network, but not when trying to control what comes from the Internet or when all the ports available need to be controlled. For example, the port for Ping (echo request) can be blocked and the internet protocol (IP) addresses would be available for the application and browsing but would not respond to Ping.

questions.text: Enterprise XYZ has outsourced production support to service provider ABC located in another country. The ABC service provider personnel remotely connect to the enterprise network of the XYZ outsourcing entity over the Internet. Which of the following would provide the BEST assurance that only authorized users of ABC connect over the Internet for production support to XYZ?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: Single sign-on (SSO) authentication
choices.justification: Single sign-on (SSO) authentication provides a single access point to system resources. It is not best in this situation.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Password complexity requirements
choices.justification: Although password complexity requirements would help prevent unauthorized access, two-factor authentication is a more effective control for this scenario.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Multifactor authentication (MFA)
choices.justification: Multifactor authentication (MFA) is the best method to provide a secure connection because it uses multiple factors, typically, what you have (e.g., a device to generate one-time passwords), what you are (e.g., biometric characteristics) or what you know (e.g., a personal identification number or password). Using a password without the use of one or more of the other factors is not the best method for this scenario.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Internet Protocol (IP) address restrictions
choices.justification: Internet Protocol (IP) addresses can always change or be spoofed and, therefore, are not the best form of authentication for the scenario.

questions.text: Which of the following will BEST maintain the integrity of a firewall log?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: Granting access to log information only to administrators
choices.justification: To enforce segregation/separation of duties (SoD), administrators should not have access to log files. This primarily contributes to the assurance of confidentiality rather than integrity.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Capturing log events in the operating system (OS) layer
choices.justification: There are many ways to capture log information—through the application layer, network layer, operating systems (OSs) layer, etc. However, there is no log integrity advantage in capturing events in the OS layer.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Writing dual logs onto separate storage media
choices.justification: If it is a highly mission-critical information system, it may be nice to run the system with a dual log mode. Having logs in two different storage devices will primarily contribute to the assurance of the availability of log information, rather than maintaining its integrity.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Sending log information to a dedicated third-party log server
choices.justification: Establishing a dedicated third-party log server and logging events in it is the best procedure for maintaining the integrity of a firewall log. When access control to the log server is adequately maintained, the risk of unauthorized log modification is mitigated, therefore improving the integrity of log information.

questions.text: Which of the following functions is performed by a virtual private network (VPN)?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: Hiding information from sniffers on the net
choices.justification: A virtual private network (VPN) hides information from sniffers on the Internet using tunneling. It works based on encapsulation and encryption of sensitive traffic.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Enforcing security policies
