choices.text: Senior management is aware of critical information assets and demonstrates an adequate concern for their protection.
choices.justification: Senior management’s level of awareness and concern for information assets is a criterion for evaluating the importance that they attach to those assets and their protection, but it is not as meaningful as having job descriptions that require all staff to be responsible for information security.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Job descriptions contain clear statements of accountability for information security.
choices.justification: The inclusion of security responsibilities in job descriptions is a key factor in demonstrating the maturity of the security program and helps ensure that staff and management are aware of their roles with respect to information security.

choices.label: C
choices.text: In accordance with the degree of risk and business impact, there is adequate funding for security efforts.
choices.justification: Funding is important but having funding does not ensure that the security program is effective or adequate.

choices.label: D
choices.text: No actual incidents have occurred that have caused a loss or a public embarrassment.
choices.justification: The number of incidents that have occurred is a criterion for evaluating the adequacy of the risk management program, but it is not a criterion for evaluating a security program.

questions.text: Which of the following controls is the MOST comprehensive in a remote access network with multiple and diverse subsystems?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate logical, physical, and environmental controls to verify the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets

choices.label: A
choices.text: Proxy server
choices.justification: A proxy server is a type of firewall installation used as an intermediary to filter and control traffic between internal and external parties.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Firewall installation
choices.justification: Although firewall installations are the primary line of defense, they would need to have encryption and a virtual private network (VPN) to secure remote access traffic.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Demilitarized zone
choices.justification: A demilitarized zone (DMZ) is an isolated network used to permit outsiders to access certain enterprise information in a semitrusted environment. The DMZ may host a web server or other external facing services. Traffic to a DMZ is not usually encrypted unless it is terminating on a VPN located in the DMZ.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Virtual private network
choices.justification: The best way to secure remote access is through the use of encrypted VPNs. This allows remote users a secure connection to the main systems.

questions.text: The management of an enterprise has decided to establish a security awareness program. Which of the following would MOST likely be a part of the program?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's security awareness training program

choices.label: A
choices.text: Using an intrusion detection system to report incidents.
choices.justification: Using an intrusion detection system to report incidents that occur is an implementation of a security program and is not effective in establishing a security awareness program.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Mandating the use of passwords to access all software.
choices.justification: Mandating the use of passwords is a policy decision, not an awareness issue.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Installing an efficient user log system to track the actions of each user.
choices.justification: Installing an efficient user log system is not a part of an awareness program.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Training provided on a regular basis to all current and new employees.
choices.justification: Regular training is an important part of a security awareness program.

questions.text: Which of the following is the BEST way for an information systems (IS) auditor to determine the effectiveness of a security awareness and training program?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's security awareness training program

choices.label: A
choices.text: the security training program.
choices.justification: A security training program may be well designed, but the results of the program are determined by employee awareness.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Ask the security administrator.
choices.justification: Asking the security administrator does not show the effectiveness of a security awareness and training program, because such a program should target more than just the administrator.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Interview a sample of employees.
choices.justification: Interviewing a sample of employees is the best way to determine the effectiveness of a security awareness and training program because overall awareness must be determined, and effective security is dependent on people. Reviewing the security training program would not be the ultimate indicator of the effectiveness of the awareness training.

choices.label: D
choices.text: the security reminders to employees.
choices.justification: the security reminders to the employees is not the best way to find out the effectiveness of the training awareness, because sending reminders may result in little actual awareness.

questions.text: Which of the following MOST effectively reduces social engineering incidents?
questions.taskStatement: Evaluate the organization's security awareness training program

choices.label: A
choices.text: Security awareness training
choices.justification: Social engineering exploits human nature and weaknesses to obtain information and access privileges. By increasing employee awareness of security issues, it is possible to reduce the number of successful social engineering incidents.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Increased physical security measures
choices.justification: In most cases, social engineering incidents do not require the physical presence of the intruder. Therefore, increased physical security measures would not prevent the incident.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Email monitoring policy
choices.justification: An email monitoring policy informs users that all email in the organization is subject to monitoring; it does not protect the users from potential security incidents and intruders.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Intrusion detection systems (IDSs)
choices.justification: Intrusion detection systems (IDSs) are used to detect irregular or abnormal traffic patterns.
